# Segment 11 — mechanism & confound investigation (phase write-up)

> Phase-specific detail. Key takeaways are also in the unified `write_up.md` "Segment 11" section.
> Chronology + data-inspection notes: `progress_log_segment_11_phase_0.md`. Consolidated verdict:
> `results/seg11_verdict.md`; per-tier: `results/seg11_{framing,mechanism,default,cap,dots,nocot}.md`;
> figures `plots/seg11_*.png`.

## Goal
Harden the EVOLVED headline for the final synthesis (Segment 12). Two motivations: (1) the Segment-10
V1 reframe ("not-told → the model banks ~1 question's worth on its DEFAULT Q1; the random-target
DISCLOSURE suppresses it") rested on **one family (Opus sumprod)** — confirm it generalizes; (2) the
genuine-hidden-computation interpretation has been "suggestive, not proven" since Segment 8 — move it
to "evidenced or honestly bounded." Plus characterize the default-target ("why Q1") and the
context-interference cap, and lightly probe the dots×k and d6 puzzles. **All EXPLORATORY mechanism work
— the locked grid/harness/scorers/preregistration are UNTOUCHED; no α-fit; no re-declaration.** Mirrors
the Segment-8 methodology (position-resolved early-pool + **Q1-only**, additive AND logit, cluster
bootstrap over instances seed 0, the reveal-before positive control as a validity guard) and re-verifies
genuine no-CoT for every new framing/level/model/filler cell.

## Setup
- Opus 4.5 (`sumprod_t3_d2`, `add_n15_d6`) + DeepSeek-V3-0324 (`add_n4_d10`, parser=open,
  provider-pinned good cluster). Minimal-reveal 4-turn harness, `sweep_positions`, counting (2 tok/item)
  + a dots probe (1 tok/item). told baselines REUSE the cached Segment-7 (Opus) / Segment-9 (DeepSeek)
  cells. The **disclose** arm (told MINUS the "— so be ready to answer any of them" clause) is built by
  post-processing `build_k_messages` (`scripts_segment_11_phase_0/run_disclosure_gen.py`, generalized
  from Segment 10 to any level/model) — the locked harness is NOT modified.
- Runner manifest `run_segment11.sh` + `scripts_segment_11_phase_0/launch_all.sh`. Analysis libs
  `seg11_lib.py` (→ seg7/8/9/10 machinery), `analyze_seg11_{nocot,framing,mechanism,default,cap,dots,
  verdict}.py`, `plot_seg11.py`, `sample_seg11.py`. Marginal spend ≈ **$409** (cumulative ≈ $2167;
  DeepSeek provider-pinned ~free; the d6/sumprod-k8 runs are the cost — long prompts).

## Gating check — genuine no-CoT holds for EVERY new cell (`results/seg11_nocot.md`)
352k k-prompt outputs across 15 new cells. **reason% = 0 everywhere** (the not-told/disclose framing
change on a NEW family (d6) and a NEW model (DeepSeek) does NOT silently enable reasoning),
**genuine-violation 0.019% overall** (worst per-file ≤0.05%, all scored WRONG), **score-inconsistent =
0**, answered-asked ~100%. The only flag is **DeepSeek not-told k=4 truncation 1.15%** — verified BENIGN
"answer-first-then-continue" (the asked answer is the FIRST token, parsed correctly; the model then
keeps answering Q2,Q3…/adds a "(Note:…)" epilogue to max_tokens; only 2/369 truncated rows are genuine
CoT; the Q1 boost is robust to dropping ALL truncated rows, +7.4→+7.8; concentrated at n=0 so if
anything conservative). The d6 not-told/disclose cells are COMPLETELY clean (0% on every metric).
Reconstructed-prompt inspection (`results/inspect_seg11.md`) confirms the not-told prompt drops the
encouragement, the disclose prompt keeps "chosen at random." but drops the encouragement, and the
fixed_item draws place the SAME revealed item across positions.

## TIER 1.1 — the not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression finding REPLICATES (cross-family + cross-model)
`results/seg11_framing.md` (+ `seg11_framing.csv`); figure ![framing](../plots/seg11_framing.png).
Undirected **Q1-only** boost B_k at k=8 (operating point), told vs disclose vs not-told:

| level / model | n0 Q1 | told | disclose | **not-told** | divided ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sumprod (Opus, original) | 20% | +1.2 | +2.0 | **+26.3 [+23.0,+29.6]** | +13.7 |
| **add_n15_d6 (Opus, cross-FAMILY)** | 48–55% | −2.2 | −3.4 | **+10.6 [+8.1,+13.1]** | +15.0 |
| **add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-MODEL)** | 53–58% | +3.4 | +2.2 | **+6.2 [+4.7,+7.8]** | +7.7 |

(k=4 agrees: d6 told +1.9 / disclose +2.2 / **not-told +12.5**; DeepSeek told +4.5 / **not-told +7.4**.)
**REPLICATES.** On BOTH the new family and the new model — as on sumprod — the model commits a positive
undirected Q1 boost ONLY under not-told, and the random-target **DISCLOSURE** (disclose ≈ told ≪
not-told) suppresses it. The magnitude tracks the **Q1 headroom** (sumprod n0 20% → +26; d6/DeepSeek n0
~50% → +6–11) and the model's overall boost size — so the smaller cross-family/cross-model magnitudes
are expected, not a failure to replicate. **Logit agrees** (not-told logit sumprod +1.23, d6 +0.43,
DeepSeek +0.27, all ≫ told). The **directedness contrast also behaves as on sumprod**: the confound-
minimal **reveal_before − disclose** test is large and framing-robust (d6 k=8 Q1 +20.0; DeepSeek k=8 Q1
+5.1), while the contrast against the not-told baseline weakens (because not-told pre-banks Q1). **The
reframe is NOT sumprod-specific.** (d6's later positions go strongly negative under not-told via
carry-disruption × recency-erasure — its regime is carry-fragile, so we lead with Q1 + logit per the
plan; the DIRECTION is unambiguous. The huge d6 late-position negatives — k=8 Q8 −60pp — are the
Tier-3.6 carry anomaly, below.)

## TIER 1.2 — genuine hidden computation vs attention/retrieval reallocation: STRENGTHENED (honestly bounded)
`results/seg11_mechanism.md` (+ `seg11_dose.csv`, `seg11_selectivity.csv`). Opus sumprod, k=8.

**(A) DIRECTED dose-response — necessary, not sufficient.** ![dose](../plots/seg11_dose.png) The directed
(reveal_before) Q1 boost RISES with filler tokens and TRACKS the lone B₁(n) shape:

| n (tok) | 24 | 50 | 100 | 150 | 200 | 300 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DIRECTED reveal_before Q1 | −1.6 | +9.7 | +21.5 | +24.5 | +25.7 | +27.1 |
| lone B₁(n) [told] | +13.3 | +23.6 | +33.5 | +35.3 | +35.5 | +33.0 |
| NOT-TOLD committed Q1 | +2.1 | +6.5 | +18.7 | +26.8 | +26.3 | +23.1 |
| lone B₁(n) [not-told] | +9.5 | +26.9 | +34.6 | +38.1 | +38.5 | +38.0 |

Both curves rise then plateau (Spearman +1.00 directed / +0.77 not-told — but Spearman over 6 points is
weak alone; the substance is the monotone-rise-then-plateau tracking the lone B₁(n)). The directed curve
tracks the lone closely; the not-told curve DIVERGES slightly at 300 tok (+26.8→+23.1 while the lone
not-told stays flat-high ~+38) — a small competing dilution term, reported not buried. A *pure-step*
attention/recency-reallocation account predicts no graded n-dependence — so the graded rise is NECESSARY
but NOT SUFFICIENT evidence (a *graded* attention/length account can also scale with n; **(B)
selectivity is the stronger lever**).

**(B) compute-selectivity (a difficulty interaction).** ![selectivity](../plots/seg11_selectivity.png)
At a fixed directed cell (reveal_before k=8, 200 tok, all positions), splitting the revealed item by
max-2×2-product quartile, the directed boost RISES with difficulty: **+6.7 → +10.8 → +14.7 → +16.2**
(logit +0.27 → +0.44 → +0.60 → +0.66) at a **~FLAT n0 baseline (52→48%)** — so it is NOT a headroom
effect; filler selectively helps the more compute-bound items. This is the k-structure analogue of
Segment 0's "filler helps deep arithmetic, not aggregation" selectivity. **Cross-check ($0, cached
directed cells):** the difficulty-selectivity REPLICATES cross-FAMILY (d6 directed boost +11.0→+19.6
with total #carries) but is **FLAT cross-MODEL** (DeepSeek ~+7 at every carry level) — so the
difficulty-selectivity is Opus-strong (sumprod max-product AND d6 carries) but does NOT clearly transfer
to the weaker open model.

**(B2) directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated (a review-driven check).** A confound:
the not-told default is positional (Q1), so "banks Q1" alone could be primacy. The **(before−after)
contrast is significantly POSITIVE at ALL 8 positions** (+7 to +27pp, every CI excludes 0) — so
directing to a NON-Q1 target does produce a directed gain everywhere; directedness is NOT merely
"position 1 is read best". **But it is strongly PRIMACY-MODULATED:** the directed *absolute* boost is
large early (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7, Q3 +20.6) and small late (Q6 +1.7, Q7 +2.4) — the model directs
compute WELL to early-listed targets and barely to Q6/Q7; the large late-position contrast there is
carried mostly by the strongly NEGATIVE reveal-after arm (recency-erasure), not a positive directed
boost. So the honest claim is **target-following AND strongly primacy-modulated** (which reinforces,
rather than separates from, the positional theme of Tier 2.3). The contrast's clean decomposition
assumes the revealed-position recency-erasure term is the same under reveal_before/after (plausible,
not airtight).

**(C) banks-vs-retrieves.** The not-told per-position profile banks **PREDOMINANTLY Q1 (+26.3)** with a
small residual early boost (Q3 +5.6, Q5 +4.5 sig.+ but ≪ the +13.7 divided prediction); the model banks
its single guessed target and retrieves it IF asked. The valid evidence against "diffuse all-questions
banking" is that the **early-mid Q2–Q5 boosts are ~+3 (≪ divided)** — NOT the late-position negatives
(recency-erasure would mask any banking there). This does NOT by itself prove the banked quantity is
arithmetic *computation* vs a maintained partial answer — that is what (A)+(B) address.

_Operating points (note): Opus uses n=200 tok for both k=4,8; DeepSeek uses k=4 @ 200 tok and k=8 @ 400
tok (holding n/k≈50 const, the Segment-9 convention) — each cell's arms share the same n, so the framing
comparison is internally clean, but cross-k/cross-model magnitudes are at slightly different n._

**Calibrated claim (sharpened after the LLM review).** **SUPPORTED:** the filler benefit is a genuine,
**dose-responsive**, **difficulty-selective**, **filler-amount-dependent** boost committed to ONE
question — the NAMED target (reveal_before, target-following per B2) or the positional default Q1
(undirected); the model does NOT spread compute across the k questions (no parallel). **UNPROVEN / honest
bounds:** (i) a **pure-step** attention-reallocation account is disfavoured (it predicts no graded
n-dependence), but a **graded** attention/bandwidth account is NOT formally excluded — a graded
"longer-context read" or a decode-time KV-buffer can also produce a monotone dose AND a difficulty
gradient at flat baseline; the difficulty-selectivity is the stronger lever but is not decisive. (ii)
**Localization is NOT established:** the data show filler ENABLES the benefit and that it scales with
filler AMOUNT, but do NOT prove the computation happens AT the filler positions vs at the decode step
reading them as scratch/KV-buffer — hence "filler-amount-dependent", NOT "filler-time". (iii) **Content
vs length:** counting is structured/numeric; the dots probe (a different filler that also banks Q1 under
not-told) shows it is not counting-specific, but both are "length" — a length-matched RANDOM filler +
activation/logit-lens probing of the last filler position are the decisive future tests (flagged for the
synthesis). (iv) **Evidence base:** the dose-response + the sumprod selectivity are Opus-sumprod-only
(selectivity also confirmed on d6 by #carries, but FLAT on DeepSeek) — the genuine-computation reading
rests mainly on Opus. We do **NOT** claim parallel latent thinking (disfavoured) and do **NOT** assert a
proven mechanism.

## TIER 2.3 — the DEFAULT-TARGET ("why Q1?") is POSITIONAL (primacy / first-listed)
`results/seg11_default.md`; figure panel ![default](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png) (a). Fixed-item
re-ordering (the SAME revealed item placed at positions {Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8}, content held FIXED, not-told
k=8): B_k = **+25.1 / −2.1 / +3.0 / −18.0** → the boost follows the listed POSITION, not the content.
Content cross-check (iid sweep, split by item difficulty): Q1 is boosted for BOTH easy (+27.7) and hard
(+24.9) items, and later positions are banked for neither → **positional/primacy default, NOT
content-based**. This directly explains the Q1-concentration in the headline.

## TIER 2.4 — the CONTEXT-INTERFERENCE CAP is mostly STRUCTURE (the first distractor)
`results/seg11_cap.md`; figure panel ![cap](../plots/seg11_default_cap.png) (b). Directed (reveal_before)
boost at a FIXED item/position-0 vs the number of distractor questions (k−1 ∈ {0,1,3,7}), 200 tok:

| k (#distractors) | 1 (0) | 2 (1) | 4 (3) | 8 (7) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| directed B_k | **+43.0** | +23.6 | +30.1 | +25.3 |
| logit | +1.84 | +0.97 | +1.25 | +1.03 |

The bulk of the cap appears IMMEDIATELY at the first distractor (k=1→k=2: +43→+24, logit +1.84→+0.97)
and is then ~flat/noisy across k=2,4,8 (a +23–30pp / +0.97–+1.25 logit band), NOT a smooth decline with
load. So the cap is **mostly imposed by the multi-question STRUCTURE (the first distractor), ~saturating
after one distractor**, with only a modest additional load component. **Lead with the LOGIT (data-
inspector):** part of the ADDITIVE drop is baseline-elevation — the n0 of the SAME fixed item at pos0
RISES with #distractors (29%→43/38/38%) because the distractors act as preceding-context filler (the
Segment-5 questions-as-filler effect), pre-consuming headroom; the logit cap (baseline-robust) shows the
SAME big-drop-at-k2-then-flat shape, so the structure-dominated conclusion holds. This BOUNDS in-prompt
latent compute: the +35–43pp lone/parallel boost is unreachable in-prompt because ANY distractor context
roughly halves the directed boost.

## TIER 3 — light probes
**3.5 dots×k (`results/seg11_dots.md`).** The dots disruption is **FRAMING-INDEPENDENT**: a ~6–7pp
uniform penalty vs counting (told +6.4, not-told +8.1) that roughly DOUBLES from k=1 (~+3.5pp lone gap)
to k=8. Under **not-told dots ALSO concentrates a large committed Q1 boost (+18.2 vs counting +26.3)** —
so dots is NOT a categorical "undelimited-run = disengagement/no-op" signal; it just is a uniformly
weaker k-structure compute substrate (plausibly a long undelimited dot run carries less positional/
structural signal than a counting sequence). The Segment-10 "dots is disruptive in the k-structure"
observation is this constant penalty viewed through the TOLD (near-zero) cell, where a negative offset
reads as "disruptive." Bounded; changes no verdict (only deepens B_k ≪ divided under told).

**3.6 d6 carry anomaly (open puzzle).** Under not-told d6's later positions go strongly negative (k=8
Q8 −60pp) — a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture far larger than sumprod's. The structured d6
per-position swing (tied to the open Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit instant-saturation puzzle) remains an OPEN
PUZZLE documented for the synthesis. It does not bear on the Q1-banking headline (we lead d6 with Q1 +
logit, where the direction is clean).

## Consolidated verdict (`results/seg11_verdict.md`)
The evolved framing headline (not-told → Q1-default banking; the random-target disclosure suppresses it)
**generalizes IN DIRECTION** cross-family (Opus d6) AND cross-model (DeepSeek) (magnitude attenuates even
on logit; DeepSeek partial). The filler benefit is **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective,
filler-amount-dependent computation** committed to a SINGLE question (the named target — target-following
but primacy-modulated; or — absent disclosure — the **positional** default Q1), **NOT parallel** and **not
a pure-step attention artifact** (a graded attention account is not formally excluded; localization not
established; the genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning); the in-prompt directed boost is **capped
at ~half the lone** by the multi-question structure (≈saturating after one distractor; partly headroom
pre-consumption). Genuine no-CoT holds throughout. All EXPLORATORY; the locked
plan/harness/scorers/pre-registration are untouched.

## Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Lead d6 + DeepSeek with Q1-only + logit (early-pool co-reported).** d6 is carry-fragile and DeepSeek
  is fast-saturating (Q2–Q4 recency-elevated), so the early pool mixes recency-confounded positions; Q1
  is the recency-free primacy read (the Segment-9/10 convention). The Q1 DIRECTION is unambiguous.
- **Disclose arm via post-processing** (not a harness edit) — keeps the locked harness untouched; the
  told/not-told cells are byte-identical to Segments 7/9/10 (cache-hit).
- **Fixed-item, position-0 for the cap** — the only position valid for k=1; holding item+position fixed
  isolates #distractors. **Fixed-item re-ordering for the default-target** — holds content fixed, varies
  position, the clean positional-vs-content test (corroborated by the iid difficulty split).
- **max-product as the difficulty proxy** — the 2×2 multiply is the compute bottleneck; it gives a boost
  gradient at a ~flat baseline (cleaner than a headroom-confounded proxy).
- **DeepSeek not-told n=0 truncation treated as benign** (answer-first-then-continue, verified) — not a
  no-CoT failure; boost robust to dropping truncated rows.
- **dots probe kept light** (one not-told k=8 cell) — enough to show the disruption is framing-
  independent, per the Tier-3 "light/optional" scope.

## Honest caveats / limitations
- **Genuine computation is STRENGTHENED, not PROVEN.** The dose-response + selectivity disfavour a
  pure-step attention account but do not formally exclude a graded attention mechanism, and do not
  separate "banked arithmetic computation" from "a maintained partial answer." We state the strongest
  SUPPORTABLE claim and stop there.
- **Cross-family/cross-model magnitudes are smaller** (d6 +11, DeepSeek +6 vs sumprod +26) — expected
  from Q1 headroom + the model's boost size; the DIRECTION + disclosure-suppression replicate, the exact
  magnitude does not transfer (and need not).
- **d6's regime is carry-fragile** (huge late-position negatives) — only its Q1/logit + contrast are
  clean; its early-pool/aggregate regime is a carry × recency mixture (an open puzzle).
- **One clean off-ceiling family per model** (the absolute-regime single-clean-family limitation carried
  from earlier segments) — the directedness/banking findings are cross-family + cross-model, but the
  absolute B_k magnitudes rest on sumprod (Opus) / add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek).
- **The context-cap is read at position 0** (primacy-disadvantaged); a middle position can't be held
  constant across k=1, so position-0 is the principled fixed point. The +23–30 band across k=2,4,8 is
  mildly non-monotone (k=4 > k=2) — reported honestly as "structure-dominated, ~saturated," not a smooth
  load curve. Part of the additive drop is baseline-elevation (distractor-as-filler raises n0) — we lead
  the cap with the baseline-robust logit.
- **Framing shifts the n0 baseline slightly even with IDENTICAL draws** (d6 told 55% vs not-told 48%;
  DeepSeek 53 vs 58, opposite sign) — a small genuine framing effect on baseline; the logit agrees and
  sumprod's baselines are EQUAL across framing yet show the largest effect, so the Q1-banking is NOT a
  headroom artifact (verified by the data-inspector). The cross-MODEL leg is the weakest (DeepSeek
  not-told vs told CIs just touch; the clean separation is not-told ≫ disclose), and the cross-FAMILY d6
  leg rests on **Q1 + logit alone** (its early-pool/aggregate is a carry×recency mixture).
