# Segment 8 — the pre-registered §5 regime + directedness verdict (Opus 4.5)

_Applies `results/preregistration.md` §5 VERBATIM to the Segment-7 data. Inference unit = the drawn INSTANCE (cluster bootstrap, 4000 resamples, seed 0; percentile CIs). Robust scorer for sumprod. Early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ listed positions; Q1-only co-reported. Comparison CI = (B_k−R̂) ± z·√(SE_Bk²+SE_R²) with SE_R the genuine cluster-bootstrap SE of the linearly-interpolated k=1 reference. δ_add=5pp; δ_logit = logit(p0+0.05)−logit(p0). Descriptive CIs are 95% (z=1.96); the §5.6 confirmatory family {sumprod k8 regime, sumprod k8 contrast, d6 k8 contrast} uses Bonferroni 98.3% CIs (z=2.39). All guards PASS (`results/seg7_guards.md`)._


## sumprod_t3_d2 + counting

### §5.1 3-way regime classification — reveal-after early-pool B_k (additive + logit)

| k | n tok | n/k | early B_k [95% CI] | additive cat | logit B_k [CI] | logit cat | div B₁(n/k) | par B₁(n) | Q1-only B_k [CI] | Q1 cat |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 100 | 50 | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** | +0.32 [+0.23,+0.41] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | +23.6 | +33.5 | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |
| 4 | 200 | 50 | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] | **NEGATIVE** | -0.20 [-0.30,-0.09] | NEGATIVE | +23.6 | +35.5 | +0.1 [-2.9,+3.2] | NULL |
| 8 | 200 | 25 | +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] | **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** | +0.14 [+0.07,+0.21] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | +13.7 | +35.5 | +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] | NULL |

_Full reveal-after n-grid (descriptive; operating point bold-faced above):_

| k | n tok | early B_k [CI] | additive cat |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 50 | +9.0 [+6.7,+11.2] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |
| 2 | 100 | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) ⟵ op |
| 2 | 200 | +6.8 [+4.5,+9.0] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |
| 4 | 100 | -9.1 [-11.1,-7.1] | NEGATIVE |
| 4 | 200 | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] | NEGATIVE ⟵ op |
| 4 | 400 | -1.6 [-3.6,+0.7] | NULL |
| 8 | 200 | +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) ⟵ op |
| 8 | 400 | +1.6 [+0.5,+2.8] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |

### §5.2 directedness contrast (before − after, paired, early pool)

| k | reveal-after B_k | reveal-before B_k (guard#1) | contrast [95% CI] | class | contrast (drop flagged rows) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | +18.8 [+16.3,+21.1] (PASS) | +10.9 [+7.8,+13.9] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +11.0 [+7.9,+13.9] |
| 4 | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] | +20.2 [+17.8,+22.5] (PASS) | +24.2 [+21.2,+27.2] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +24.2 [+21.2,+27.2] |
| 8 | +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] | +18.7 [+17.2,+20.3] (PASS) | +16.2 [+14.3,+18.3] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +16.5 [+14.5,+18.5] |

## add_n15_d6 + counting

### §5.1 3-way regime classification — reveal-after early-pool B_k (additive + logit)

| k | n tok | n/k | early B_k [95% CI] | additive cat | logit B_k [CI] | logit cat | div B₁(n/k) | par B₁(n) | Q1-only B_k [CI] | Q1 cat |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 100 | 50 | +0.5 [-1.2,+2.4] | **NULL** | +0.02 [-0.05,+0.10] | NULL | +24.5 | +29.2 | +0.5 [-1.2,+2.4] | NULL |
| 4 | 200 | 50 | -1.4 [-3.1,+0.4] | **NULL** | -0.05 [-0.13,+0.02] | NULL | +24.5 | +28.1 | +1.9 [-0.4,+4.3] | NULL |
| 8 | 200 | 25 | -16.8 [-18.2,-15.3] | **NEGATIVE** | -0.68 [-0.74,-0.62] | NEGATIVE | +15.0 | +28.1 | -2.2 [-4.7,+0.3] | NULL |

_Full reveal-after n-grid (descriptive; operating point bold-faced above):_

| k | n tok | early B_k [CI] | additive cat |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 50 | -1.2 [-2.8,+0.4] | NULL |
| 2 | 100 | +0.5 [-1.2,+2.4] | NULL ⟵ op |
| 2 | 200 | +2.1 [+0.1,+3.9] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |
| 4 | 100 | -3.4 [-5.1,-1.7] | NEGATIVE |
| 4 | 200 | -1.4 [-3.1,+0.4] | NULL ⟵ op |
| 4 | 400 | +3.1 [+1.2,+5.0] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) |
| 8 | 200 | -16.8 [-18.2,-15.3] | NEGATIVE ⟵ op |
| 8 | 400 | -12.1 [-13.6,-10.6] | NEGATIVE |

### §5.2 directedness contrast (before − after, paired, early pool)

| k | reveal-after B_k | reveal-before B_k (guard#1) | contrast [95% CI] | class | contrast (drop flagged rows) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | +0.5 [-1.2,+2.4] | +15.7 [+13.8,+17.6] (PASS) | +15.2 [+12.7,+17.7] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +15.2 [+12.7,+17.7] |
| 4 | -1.4 [-3.1,+0.4] | +20.4 [+18.3,+22.4] (PASS) | +21.8 [+18.9,+24.4] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +21.8 [+18.9,+24.4] |
| 8 | -16.8 [-18.2,-15.3] | +15.1 [+13.9,+16.4] (PASS) | +31.9 [+30.0,+33.8] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** | +31.9 [+30.0,+33.8] |

## §5.6 confirmatory family (Bonferroni 98.3% CIs / z=2.39 — EXACTLY 3 pre-declared tests)

_Pre-declared family (CONF_FAMILY): [('add_n15_d6', 8, 'contrast'), ('sumprod_t3_d2', 8, 'contrast'), ('sumprod_t3_d2', 8, 'regime')]._

| test | statistic [98.3% CI] | confirmatory verdict |
|---|---|---|
| sumprod k=8 **regime** (additive) | B_k = +2.5 [+0.9,+4.0] | **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** (excl null+divided) |
| sumprod k=8 regime (logit confirm) | +0.14 [+0.05,+0.22] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) (agrees) |
| sumprod k=8 **contrast** | +16.2 [+13.9,+18.8] | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** (before ctrl +18.7 [+16.9,+20.6]) |
| d6 k=8 **contrast** (cross-family, LOGIT = formal test) | logit +1.50 [+1.39,+1.60] (before +0.81 vs after -0.68) | **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** |
| d6 k=8 contrast (additive, context only) | +31.9 [+29.6,+34.2] | DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE — magnitude INFLATED by the headroom-limited before (Q4 95%) + the recency-confounded after-arm (−16.8); the SIGN is the cross-family takeaway, NOT the magnitude |

## §5.7 fresh-only firewall + §5.9 qualifier gates (headline = sumprod k=8 n=200)

- **Full-sample additive early-pool** B_k = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] → **PARTIAL (sub-divided)**; **logit** +0.14 [+0.07,+0.21] → PARTIAL (sub-divided).
- **§5.1 comparison CIs (auditable; SE_Bk=0.63pp):** vs null [+1.2,+3.7] (EXCLUDES 0); vs divided +13.7 (SE_R 0.91pp) → gap [-13.4,-9.0]pp, **10.1σ** (SE_R-inclusive, NOT the fixed-reference design-power σ); vs parallel +35.5 (SE_R 1.01pp) → gap [-35.4,-30.7]pp, 27.8σ. NB the divided ref B₁(25) sits on the steep rising edge near n_lo so SE_R could be under-estimated, but the ~11pp gap ≫ any plausible interpolation error → the divided EXCLUSION is robust (a divided CLASSIFICATION would have needed full curve-shape uncertainty propagated; not at issue here).
- **(gate 1) logit vs additive:** AGREE (additive PARTIAL, logit PARTIAL).
- **(gate 2) two-way (instance+item) bootstrap:** B_k = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] (SE_2way 0.65pp = √(SE_inst²0.63²+SE_item²0.63²−SE_iid²0.62²)) → PARTIAL (sub-divided); category UNCHANGED.
- **(gate 3) fresh-only** (inst>649, n=350): B_k = +0.9 [-1.1,+2.9] → **NULL**; contrast +18.8 [+15.4,+22.1] (before ctrl +19.7 [+17.1,+22.3]). DISAGREES → append “not robust to held-out”. **Power limitation (explicit):** the never-seen portion is only ~350 instances → the fresh CI is ±~3pp, a WEAK firewall that confirms B_k ≪ divided but cannot crisply separate NULL from PARTIAL; its CI [-1.1,+2.9] still COVERS the full +2.5 → power-limited NON-replication, NOT refutation. Pre-registered anomaly REVERSED: the pilot fresh subset sat ABOVE the full sample (+5.0 vs +3.3); on the full run fresh (+0.9) sits BELOW full (+2.5).
- **(gate 4) Q1-only:** B_k = +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] → **NULL**; DIFFERS → append “position-sensitive”.

**§5.9 headline label:** additive early-pool point estimate = **PARTIAL (sub-divided)**, qualifiers: _not robust to held-out; position-sensitive_.

## §5.5 combined interpretation + §5.8 headline declaration

- regime axis (§5.1): **PARTIAL** (PARTIAL (sub-divided)); directedness axis (§5.2): **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** → combined: **Partial directed — a small undirected benefit ≪ directed, ≪ divided**.

> **HEADLINE (§5.8):** At the headline cell (sumprod, k=8, n=200), the early-position reveal-after B_k is **PARTIAL (sub-divided)** (95% CI [+1.2,+3.7]pp additive, agreeing on the logit scale), and the directedness contrast is **DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** (CI [+14.3,+18.3]pp, with a significantly-positive reveal-before control [+17.2,+20.3]pp), giving the combined interpretation **Partial directed — a small undirected benefit ≪ directed, ≪ divided**. Qualifiers (§5.9): _not robust to held-out; position-sensitive_ — the full-sample additive early-pool is the point estimate; on Q1-only and the held-out subsample the regime reads NULL (both far below divided), so the precise NULL-vs-PARTIAL split is not robust, while the directedness conclusion and B_k ≪ divided are.** NB 'PARTIAL' here is statistically non-null (CI excludes 0 even at 98.3%) but scientifically NEAR-zero (~7% of B₁, ≪ divided) and fragile — NOT 'appreciable partial banking'; the held-out CI [-1.1,+2.9] still COVERS the full +2.5 (power-limited non-replication, not refutation).

_Contrast with vs without guard-#4 flagged rows: +16.2 [+14.3,+18.3] vs +16.5 [+14.5,+18.5] (immaterial)._

### Per-k picture (descriptive, NON-monotone; co-report Q1-only)

| k | early B_k [CI] | category | Q1-only B_k [CI] | Q1 category | note |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | +7.8 [+5.5,+10.1] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | least-shared |
| 4 | -4.0 [-6.2,-1.8] | NEGATIVE | +0.1 [-2.9,+3.2] | NULL | early pool incl. transitional Q2 → position-sensitive |
| 8 | +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7] | PARTIAL (sub-divided) | +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6] | NULL | HEADLINE; clean Q1–Q4 |

## Supplementary within-scope diagnostics on the small headline +2.5 (mechanism of the near-null positive)

- **(1) Fixed-item corr(B_k, B₁)** (same revealed item alone at k=1 vs shared at k=8, n=100it; the exact item+control arm): Pearson r = **+0.005 [-0.058,+0.070]** over 1000 items → ≈ 0. **Largely UNINFORMATIVE, not directional evidence:** a per-item k=1 boost is a single Bernoulli difference (∈{−1,0,+1}), so any true latent correlation is heavily ATTENUATED toward 0 by measurement error regardless of mechanism — the tight CI rules out a large OBSERVED correlation, not a substantial latent one. Consistent with either reading; it only tells us the per-item structure of B₁ is not strongly visible in the noisy k=8 boost.
- **(2) k-gradient at matched per-question filler n/k=25 tok:** k=2 early B_k(=Q1) = +9.0 [+6.7,+11.2] vs k=8 early B_k(=Q1–Q4) = +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7]. The boost is LARGER at the LESS-shared k=2, which is what sharing-limited genuine banking predicts. **At most WEAK/AMBIGUOUS evidence on the artifact, though:** an attention-redistribution artifact (caveat iv) could ALSO decrease with k — at higher k the reallocated attention is spread over a larger early pool (Q1 at k=2 vs Q1–Q4 at k=8), pushing the per-position artifact DOWN with k — so the artifact's k-dependence is itself ambiguous. (Composition note: k=2's early pool IS Q1, so this is ~a Q1-vs-Q1–Q4 read; the Q1-vs-Q1 read +9.0 vs +1.2 is even steeper. k=4 omitted — its early pool includes the transitional Q2, position-confounded.)
- **(3) Early-pool DEFINITION sensitivity (headline k=8, n=200):** Q1 +1.2 [-1.1,+3.6]; Q1–2 +2.6 [+0.9,+4.4]; Q1–3 +2.5 [+1.0,+3.9]; Q1–4 +2.5 [+1.2,+3.7]. The early-pool point estimate is ~stable (+2 to +2.5) across the boundary choice; the Q1-only → NULL split is a POWER difference (Q1-only has ¼ the rows, CI just covers 0), NOT a genuine within-band position dependence — the ⌈k/2⌉ boundary is not driving the result.

**Net on the +2.5 mechanism (analysis-only):** UNRESOLVED — the fixed-item corr≈0 is largely uninformative (attenuation), and the k-gradient is at most weak/ambiguous on the artifact; neither decisively bears either way. The attention-redistribution artifact (caveat iv) remains a LIVE possibility alongside weak genuine sharing-limited banking. The robust conclusions (B_k ≪ divided at ~10σ; DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE) do NOT depend on resolving it. A definitive attention/mechanism separation is a Segment-11 deep-dive.

# Segment 9 — DeepSeek cross-model verdict (directedness contrast PRIMARY)

Level **add_n4_d10**, parser=open, provider-pinned good cluster, told framing, counting filler, minimal reveal, sweep_positions (position-resolved). Cluster bootstrap over instances (seed 0, nb=4000). `n` = measured input_tokens delta.

k=1 in-structure n=0 baseline = 55.8%; B1 ~+7-8pp plateau (+8.7pp @400 tok; densified m=4000). Modest boost -> parallel/divided refs CLOSE (weak 3-way power) -> lead with the contrast.


⚑ **Pool choice (data-motivated, exploratory — NOT pre-specified):** add_n4_d10 is fast-saturating, so Q2-Q4 are recency-ELEVATED at n=0 (Q1 ~54%, Q2-Q4 ~70-76%) — only **Q1** is recency-free (cf. Opus sumprod's flat 20-25% early band). The undirected reveal_after EARLY pool is therefore recency-confounded (filler erases Q2-Q4's recency -> drags it negative). We LEAD with **Q1-only as the least-recency-contaminated read** and co-report the Opus-matched early pool as a sensitivity arm. Caveat: Q1 has its OWN primacy/low-baseline headroom, so 'clean' = 'recency-free', not 'artifact-free'. The directedness conclusion holds on BOTH pools + the sig.+ positive control.

## PRIMARY — directedness contrast (reveal_before − reveal_after)

Each B_k net of its OWN n=0 control. Positive control = reveal_before B_k (must be sig.+).

| k | op n (tok) | pool | after B_k | **before B_k (ctrl)** | **contrast (pp)** | class | contrast logit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | 199 | early | +8.2 [+6.4,+10.1] | +7.9 [+6.0,+9.8] (sig.+) | **-0.3 [-2.8,+2.1]** | NO-DIRECTEDNESS-EFFECT | +0.00 [-0.10,+0.10] |
| 2 | 199 | Q1 | +8.2 [+6.4,+10.1] | +7.9 [+6.0,+9.8] (sig.+) | **-0.3 [-2.8,+2.1]** | NO-DIRECTEDNESS-EFFECT | +0.00 [-0.10,+0.10] |
| 4 | 199 | early | +2.1 [+1.1,+3.0] | +7.2 [+6.3,+8.1] (sig.+) | **+5.1 [+3.9,+6.4]** | DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE | +0.25 [+0.20,+0.31] |
| 4 | 199 | Q1 | +4.5 [+3.3,+5.8] | +7.1 [+5.9,+8.4] (sig.+) | **+2.6 [+0.8,+4.3]** | DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE | +0.11 [+0.04,+0.18] |
| 8 | 399 | early | -1.5 [-2.1,-0.8] | +7.4 [+6.9,+8.0] (sig.+) | **+8.9 [+8.0,+9.7]** | DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE | +0.48 [+0.44,+0.52] |
| 8 | 399 | Q1 | +3.4 [+2.1,+4.7] | +5.3 [+4.1,+6.6] (sig.+) | **+1.9 [+0.2,+3.6]** | DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE | +0.09 [+0.01,+0.15] |

## Cross-k contrast TREND test (Q1-only) — does the directedness effect emerge/grow with k?

contrast(k) = before_Bk − after_Bk (Q1-only). The k cells are independent draws -> the
k-vs-k difference is an UNPAIRED bootstrap-distribution difference (NOT 'separate
significance'). Tests whether the contrast is larger at higher k.

| comparison | Δ contrast (pp) [CI] | |
|---|---|---|

## HEADLINE — does the Opus picture replicate on DeepSeek?

**PARTIALLY REPLICATES (a weaker, task-limited replication).** The qualitative Opus picture reproduces: (1) a **directedness contrast that EMERGES at k≥4** (≈0 at k=2), positive and sig.+ on BOTH the least-recency-contaminated Q1-only read (k2 −0.3 [-2.8,+2.1] -> k4 +2.6 [+0.8,+4.3] -> k8 +1.9 [+0.2,+3.6]) and the Opus-matched early pool (+5.1 -> +8.9; this one GROWS with k, but is recency-amplified), with a **robustly sig.+ reveal_before positive control at every k**. (The cross-k trend on Q1 is suggestive but NOT formally significant: contrast(k=4)−contrast(k=2) = +2.9pp [-0.0,+5.7], contrast(k=8)−contrast(k=2) = +2.2 [-0.7,+5.0]; the early-pool trend IS significant but recency-amplified — so 'emerges at k≥4' is the honest read, 'grows with k' only on the early pool.) (2) the **undirected boost DECLINES with k and is sig. below divided at k=4 AND k=8** (Q1 B_k k4 +4.5 / k8 +3.4 — ~55% / ~39% of B1; B_k−divided = −3.1 [-5.2,-1.1] / −4.3 [-6.3,-2.2]; logit agrees; the early pool even goes slightly NEGATIVE at k=8 via recency-erasure of Q2-Q4 — the questions-as-filler effect). The cleanest cross-k view holds TOTAL filler FIXED at 199 tok: Q1 undirected falls **+8.2→+4.5→+1.7** (k=2→4→8) — a clean divided-pool-shaped decline (sig. below k=2 by k=8), falling below even the divided prediction by k=8. BUT honest cross-model differences: (a) **k=2 has NO directedness penalty** (contrast ≈0, robust + tight CI + sig.+ control — NOT a power artifact) whereas Opus penalizes undirected even at k=2 (contrast +10.9). NB at k=2 the saturated curve makes divided B1(n/2)=+6.7 ≈ parallel +8.2, so the FULL undirected retention (Q1 +8.2 ≈101%) does NOT distinguish parallel from divided — it only says DeepSeek banks the undirected boost intact at low sharing. (b) the **modest ~+6-9pp boost makes parallel≈divided**, so the 3-way regime is power-limited and NOT crisply resolvable (graceful degradation — α NOT fit, no forced label). The contrast is also ~6x smaller than Opus by boost magnitude (Q1 contrast ~+2 vs Opus ~+25pp — ~8-9x).

⚠ **OVERARCHING caveat — cross-MODEL is confounded with cross-LEVEL.** Opus ran on the gradual `sumprod` (flat low early band) and DeepSeek on the fast-saturating `add_n4_d10` (recency-elevated Q2-Q4) — DIFFERENT levels (no DeepSeek level matched sumprod's profile; only add_n4_d10 boosts among addition levels, and a gradual non-addition DeepSeek level was not calibrated, out of scope). So EVERY cross-model difference here (incl. the k=2≈0 and the smaller magnitude) is confounded with level/saturation and cannot be cleanly attributed to the MODEL. The tight k=2 CI rules out a power artifact, NOT a level effect. This is the headline caveat of the whole cross-model read.

⚠ **Interpretation caution (mirrors the Opus caveat):** a positive reveal-before−after contrast shows a **target-conditioned advantage** (knowing the target before the filler helps), which is SUGGESTIVE of directed hidden computation but does NOT uniquely prove it — alternatives include attention/routing to the known target, easier maintenance of one answer vs several, reveal-after index-selection overhead, and position/recency interactions. The robust, falsifiable claim is the asymmetry itself + the undirected≪divided-at-k8 read, NOT a proven compute-allocation mechanism.

**Transfers (design/scoring/the qualitative finding):** the reveal-by-index apparatus, minimal-reveal, position-resolution, the directedness contrast + sig.+ positive control, the questions-as-filler / recency-erasure phenomenology, and the qualitative directedness-positive + undirected-declining-with-k + ≪divided-at-k8 picture.

**Model-specific (does NOT transfer):** the operating point / mid-band level (add_n4_d10 @ ~56% vs sumprod @ ~34%), the boost magnitude (~+6 vs ~+35pp), the realized-token mapping, the recency profile (DeepSeek recency-elevates Q2-Q4 so only Q1 is clean), the k=2 full-retention (vs Opus sub-divided at k=2), and the crispness of the 3-way regime read.

## TIER 1.1 — the not-told Q1-default-banking + disclosure-suppression finding REPLICATES (in DIRECTION) cross-FAMILY (Opus d6) AND cross-MODEL (DeepSeek)

Undirected Q1 boost B_k at k=8 (operating point), told vs disclose vs not-told:

| level / model | told | disclose | not-told | divided ref | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sumprod (Opus, original) | +1.2 | +2.0 | **+26.3** [+23.0,+29.6] | +13.7 | not-told ≫ disclose ≈ told ✓ |
| add_n15_d6 (Opus, cross-family) | -2.2 | -3.4 | **+10.6** [+8.1,+13.1] | +15.0 | not-told ≫ disclose ≈ told ✓ |
| add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-model) | +3.4 | +2.2 | **+6.2** [+4.7,+7.8] | +7.7 | not-told > disclose ≈ told ✓ |

**REPLICATES (in DIRECTION).** On the NEW family (d6) and the NEW model (DeepSeek) — as on the original sumprod — the model commits a positive Q1 boost ONLY under not-told, and the random-target DISCLOSURE (disclose ≈ told) suppresses it. The reframe is **NOT sumprod-specific**. **But the magnitude ATTENUATES cross-family and especially cross-model even on the headroom-robust LOGIT scale** (not-told−told Δlogit: sumprod +1.16 ≫ d6 +0.52 ≫ DeepSeek +0.12) — the smaller cross-family/model effect is only PARTLY headroom (the additive numbers sumprod +26 / d6 +11 / DeepSeek +6 partly reflect Q1 headroom, but DeepSeek is ~10× weaker even on logit). **Honest scope (data-inspector):** the cleanest separation everywhere is **not-told ≫ disclose**; on DeepSeek not-told vs told CIs just touch (a weaker, partial cross-model leg, consistent with Segment 9). The **d6 leg rests on Q1 + logit alone** (its early-pool/aggregate is uninterpretable: later positions go to −60pp via carry-disruption×recency-erasure, carry-fragile regime); the DIRECTION is unambiguous (told −2.2/logit −0.09 -> not-told +10.6/logit +0.43). **Framing also shifts n0 slightly even with IDENTICAL draws** (d6 told 55% vs not-told 48%; DeepSeek 53 vs 58, opposite sign), but logit agrees and sumprod's baselines are EQUAL across framing yet show the LARGEST effect — so the Q1-banking is NOT a headroom artifact.)

## TIER 1.2 — genuine-hidden-computation vs attention/retrieval reallocation: STRENGTHENED (not proven, honestly bounded)

- **(A) DIRECTED dose-response (necessary, not sufficient):** the directed (reveal_before) Q1 boost RISES with filler tokens (−1.6→+27.1 over 24→300 tok) and TRACKS the lone B1(n) shape; the not-told committed Q1 boost likewise scales. A *pure-step* attention/recency-reallocation account predicts no graded n-dependence -> the graded rise DISFAVOURS a pure-step account, but a *graded* attention/length account can also scale with n, so this is necessary not sufficient (selectivity (B) is the stronger lever).
- **(B) compute-selectivity:** at a fixed directed cell the boost RISES with item difficulty (sumprod max-product quartile +6.7→+10.8→+14.7→+16.2; logit +0.27→+0.66) at a ~FLAT n0 baseline (52→48%) -> NOT headroom; filler selectively helps the more compute-bound items. **Cross-check ($0, cached directed cells, WITH the flat-baseline control):** the directed boost RISES with #carries on d6 (+11.0→+19.6) BUT d6's n0 also DECLINES ~8pp (74→66%) — a partial headroom confound (unlike sumprod's flat baseline); on DeepSeek the baseline IS flat but the boost is ALSO flat (~+7, no selectivity). So the CLEANEST difficulty-selectivity is sumprod (rising boost AT a flat baseline); the genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning, not uniformly cross-family/model clean.
- **(B2) directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated:** the (before−after) contrast is sig.+ at ALL 8 positions (+7 to +27pp), so directing to a NON-Q1 target gains everywhere (not merely 'position 1 is read best'); BUT the directed *absolute* boost is large early (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7, Q3 +20.6) and small at Q6/Q7 (+1.7/+2.4) — so directed efficacy is strongly primacy-graded (the large late-position contrast is carried by the negative reveal-after arm). The not-told Q1 default is positional because Q1 is the best GUESS when the model must commit before knowing the target.
- **(C) banks-vs-retrieves:** the not-told per-position profile is Q1 +26.3, Q2–Q5 ~+3, Q6–Q8 negative -> the model banks a SINGLE guessed target (its default Q1) and retrieves it IF asked; the small reveal-after AVERAGE is because the reveal is random. This REFUTES 'banks a diffuse all-questions computation it cannot retrieve'.
- **SUPPORTED:** genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, **filler-amount-dependent** computation committed to ONE question — the NAMED target (reveal_before, target-following) or the **positional default Q1** (undirected). The model does NOT spread compute across the k questions (no parallel). **UNPROVEN / honest bounds (sharpened after LLM review):** (i) a PURE-STEP attention account is disfavoured (no graded n-dependence) but a GRADED attention/bandwidth ('longer-context read' or decode-time KV-buffer) account is NOT formally excluded — it can also yield a monotone dose + a difficulty gradient at flat baseline; selectivity is the stronger lever, not decisive. (ii) LOCALIZATION is not established: the data show filler ENABLES the benefit and that it scales with filler AMOUNT, but not that the computation happens AT the filler positions vs at decode reading them as scratch — hence 'filler-amount-dependent', NOT 'filler-time'. (iii) CONTENT vs length: dots (a different filler) also banks Q1 under not-told, so it is not counting-specific, but both are 'length' (a random-filler control + activation probing are the decisive future tests). (iv) **EVIDENCE BASE:** the dose-response + the CLEAN (flat-baseline) selectivity are Opus-sumprod-only (on d6 the selectivity rises but with a baseline confound; on DeepSeek it is absent) — the genuine-computation interpretation rests mainly on Opus. We do NOT claim parallel latent thinking and do NOT assert a proven mechanism.

## TIER 2.3 — the DEFAULT-TARGET (why Q1?) is POSITIONAL (primacy / first-listed)

Fixed-item re-ordering (SAME item at positions {Q1,Q2,Q4,Q8}, not-told k=8): B_k = +25.1 / −2.1 / +3.0 / −18.0 -> the boost follows the listed POSITION, not the content. Content cross-check (iid): Q1 boosted for BOTH easy (+27.7) and hard (+24.9) items, later positions banked for neither -> **positional/primacy default, NOT content-based**.

## TIER 2.4 — the CONTEXT-INTERFERENCE CAP is mostly STRUCTURE (the first distractor)

Directed (reveal_before) boost at a FIXED item/position vs #distractors (200 tok): lone (k=1) +43.0 -> k=2 +23.6 -> k=4 +30.1 -> k=8 +25.3 (logit +1.84 -> +0.97/+1.25/+1.03; LEAD with logit). The bulk of the cap appears IMMEDIATELY at the first distractor and then ~saturates -> the cap is **mostly the multi-question STRUCTURE, not progressive load**. Two honest refinements (reviewers): (i) part of the cap is **headroom pre-consumption** — the distractor questions act as preceding-context filler, raising the pos0 n0 (29%->38%) even at n=0; (ii) in REACHABLE-ACCURACY terms the gap is modest (in-prompt directed Q1 reaches ~63% = 38+25 vs lone ~72% = 29+43, ~9pp), so 'roughly halves the boost' is a boost/logit-space statement — the cleaner mechanism is 'part of the budget is already spent on questions-as-filler'.

## TIER 3 — light probes

- **dots×k (3.5):** the dots disruption is FRAMING-INDEPENDENT, a ~6-7pp uniform penalty vs counting that roughly doubles from k=1 (~+3.5pp gap) to k=8. Under not-told dots ALSO concentrates a large committed Q1 boost (+18.2 vs counting +26.3) -> dots is NOT a categorical 'no-op/disengagement' signal, just a weaker k-structure compute substrate. Bounded; does not change any verdict (only deepens B_k ≪ divided under told).
- **d6 carry anomaly (3.6):** under not-told d6's later positions go strongly negative (k=8 Q8 −60pp) — a carry-disruption × recency-erasure mixture far larger than sumprod's; the structured d6 per-position swing remains an OPEN PUZZLE (tied to the Segment-2 8-vs-6-digit saturation anomaly), documented for the synthesis. It does not bear on the Q1-banking headline (we lead d6 with Q1 + logit).

## Net

The evolved framing headline (not-told -> Q1-default banking; disclosure suppresses it) **generalizes IN DIRECTION** cross-family + cross-model (magnitude attenuates even on logit; DeepSeek partial); the filler benefit is **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent computation** committed to a SINGLE question (the named target — target-following but primacy-modulated; or the positional default Q1), **NOT parallel**, **not a pure-step attention artifact** (though a graded attention account is not formally excluded and localization is not established); the default target is **positional**; the in-prompt directed boost is **capped at ~half the lone** by the multi-question structure (one distractor). All EXPLORATORY; genuine no-CoT holds throughout.

# Segment 11 — TIER 1.2: genuine-hidden-computation vs attention/retrieval reallocation (Opus sumprod_t3_d2, k=8)

## (A) Directed + not-told dose-response (necessary, not sufficient)

_Rules out a present/absent STEP account, but a GRADED attention/length account can also scale with n — so this is necessary, not sufficient; (B) selectivity is the stronger lever._

Q1 boost vs realized filler tokens (k=8). Lone B1(n) = the single-question curve (told from seg7 k=1; not-told from seg10 k=1).

| n (tok) | DIRECTED reveal_before Q1 | lone B1(n) [told] | NOT-TOLD Q1 | lone B1(n) [not-told] |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 24 | -1.6 | +13.3 | +2.1 | +9.5 |
| 50 | +9.7 | +23.6 | +6.5 | +26.9 |
| 100 | +21.5 | +33.5 | +18.7 | +34.6 |
| 150 | +24.5 | +35.3 | +26.8 | +38.1 |
| 200 | +25.7 | +35.5 | +26.3 | +38.5 |
| 300 | +27.1 | +33.0 | +23.1 | +38.0 |

**Both curves RISE with filler amount then plateau** (Spearman rank-corr directed +1.00, not-told +0.77 — note Spearman over 6 points is weak as a standalone stat; the substance is the monotone-rise-then-plateau tracking the lone B1(n)). The directed curve tracks the lone closely; the not-told curve dips at 300 tok (+26.8->+23.1) while the lone not-told stays flat-high (+38) — a small DIVERGENCE (a competing dilution term), reported not buried. A present/absent STEP would show no graded n-dependence; the graded rise is necessary (not sufficient) evidence for filler-amount-dependent computation.


## (B) Compute-selectivity — directed boost vs item difficulty (a-priori proxy)

Directed cell = reveal_before, k=8, n=200 tok (size 100), ALL positions (each directed). Split the revealed item by max-product quartile (the 2x2 multiply is the compute bottleneck). Genuine arithmetic computation should help HARDER items more.

| difficulty quartile (max product) | n0 acc | directed B_k (add) | logit B_k | n |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 [770-3239] | 52% | +6.7 [+4.7,+8.6] | +0.27 | 1998 |
| Q2 [3239-4692] | 49% | +10.8 [+8.6,+12.9] | +0.44 | 1988 |
| Q3 [4692-6232] | 49% | +14.7 [+12.5,+16.9] | +0.60 | 2009 |
| Q4 [6232-9702] | 48% | +16.2 [+13.9,+18.4] | +0.66 | 2005 |

**Hardest vs easiest quartile:** additive +16.2 vs +6.7 (Δ +9.5); logit +0.66 vs +0.27. _(n0 is ~flat across quartiles (52->48%), so the rising boost is NOT a headroom effect; the logit gap confirms it.)_

### (B) cross-family/model selectivity check (cached directed cells; $0)

Re-running the difficulty split on the CACHED directed (reveal_before) cells of the other family (d6, by total #carries) and the other model (DeepSeek add_n4_d10, by #carries). Genuine arithmetic computation should help harder items more here too.

| level / model | directed B_k by carry-quartile (low->high) | n0 by quartile | rises? |
|---|---|---|---|
| d6 (Opus, cross-FAMILY) | +11.0 -> +15.8 -> +18.1 -> +19.6 | 74/69/68/66% | **YES** (with a baseline confound) |
| add_n4_d10 (DeepSeek, cross-MODEL) | +7.9 -> +6.6 -> +6.1 -> +7.9 | 77/77/78/76% | **NO** (flat ~+7) |

**Honest cross-check (with the flat-baseline control):** the directed boost RISES with #carries on d6 (+11.0->+19.6), BUT — unlike sumprod's ~flat baseline — d6's n0 DECLINES ~7pp across carry-quartiles (74->66%), so the d6 rise is PARTLY headroom, not a clean flat-baseline selectivity. On DeepSeek the baseline IS flat (~77%) but the boost is ALSO flat (~+7) — NO difficulty-selectivity. **So the CLEANEST difficulty-selectivity is sumprod (rising boost AT a flat baseline); d6 rises with a baseline confound; DeepSeek shows none.** The genuine-computation evidence is Opus-leaning and not uniformly cross-family/model clean — a real bound, not a clean replication.


## (B2) Directedness is target-following AND primacy-modulated (not pure primacy, but strongly primacy-graded)

A key confound (raised in review): the not-told DEFAULT target is positional (Q1), so 'banks Q1' alone could be primacy, not selective direction. Rebuttal: when DIRECTED (reveal_before names a NON-Q1 target before the filler), the boost FOLLOWS the named target. Per-position directed boost + the (before−after) contrast at k=8, 200 tok:

| position | n0 | directed reveal_before B_k | reveal_after B_k | contrast (before−after) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | 36% | +25.7 | +1.2 | +24.5 [+20.5,+28.5] |
| Q2 | 48% | +18.7 | +4.0 | +14.7 [+10.8,+18.7] |
| Q3 | 40% | +20.6 | +2.2 | +18.4 [+14.5,+22.4] |
| Q4 | 52% | +9.8 | +2.5 | +7.3 [+3.5,+11.1] |
| Q5 | 46% | +8.1 | -4.4 | +12.5 [+8.8,+16.3] |
| Q6 | 60% | +1.7 | -15.9 | +17.6 [+13.5,+21.6] |
| Q7 | 61% | +2.4 | -22.8 | +25.2 [+21.2,+29.3] |
| Q8 | 53% | +9.6 | -17.3 | +26.9 [+22.7,+31.1] |

**The (before−after) contrast is significantly POSITIVE at ALL 8 positions** (+7 to +27pp, every CI excludes 0), so directing to a NON-Q1 target produces a directed gain everywhere — directedness is NOT merely 'position 1 is read best'. **But the effect is strongly PRIMACY-MODULATED:** the directed *absolute* boost is large at early positions (Q1 +25.7, Q2 +18.7, Q3 +20.6) and small at Q6/Q7 (+1.7/+2.4; Q8 ticks back up to +9.6, a final-position recency uptick) — the model directs compute WELL to early-listed targets and barely to Q6/Q7. The large late-position CONTRAST there is carried mostly by the strongly NEGATIVE reveal-after arm (recency-erasure Q7 after −22.8), not by a positive directed boost. So the honest claim is **target-following AND strongly primacy-modulated** (which reinforces, rather than separates from, the positional theme of Tier 2.3). The contrast's clean decomposition assumes the recency-erasure term at the revealed position is the same under reveal_before and reveal_after (plausible — filler mechanically erases late-position salience regardless of framing — but not airtight). The not-told Q1-DEFAULT (Tier 2.3) is positional because Q1 is the model's best GUESS when it must commit before knowing the target.


## (C) Banks-vs-retrieves — the not-told per-position reveal-after profile

| position | n0 acc | not-told reveal-after B_k |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | 20% | +26.3 [+23.0,+29.6] |
| Q2 | 28% | +1.0 [-1.8,+3.8] |
| Q3 | 28% | +5.6 [+3.0,+8.3] |
| Q4 | 32% | +0.9 [-1.7,+3.5] |
| Q5 | 24% | +4.5 [+2.1,+6.9] |
| Q6 | 47% | -17.8 [-20.9,-14.7] |
| Q7 | 55% | -25.4 [-28.7,-22.2] |
| Q8 | 47% | -19.0 [-22.4,-15.9] |

**Q1 banked-and-retrieved = +26.3; the mean of Q2-Q5 = +3.0** (small, with Q3 +5.6 / Q5 +4.5 sig.+ but ≪ the +13.7 divided prediction). The model banks PREDOMINANTLY its single guessed Q1 (a small residual early-position boost remains) and retrieves it IF Q1 is the one asked; because the reveal is random the reveal-after AVERAGE is small. The valid evidence against 'diffuse all-questions banking' is that the EARLY-MID Q2-Q5 boosts are ~+3 (≪ divided) — NOT the late-position negatives (which recency-erasure would mask any banking at). This does NOT prove the banked quantity is arithmetic *computation* vs a maintained partial answer — that is what (A)+(B) address.


## Calibrated claim: what is SUPPORTED vs what stays UNPROVEN

- **SUPPORTED:** the filler benefit is a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent** boost committed to ONE question — the NAMED target (reveal_before, the boost follows it to any position, B2) or, absent the random-target disclosure, the **positional default Q1**. It scales with the AMOUNT of filler (not a present/absent step) and selectively helps the more compute-bound items at a flat baseline. The model does NOT spread compute across the k questions (no parallel).
- **UNPROVEN / honest bounds (sharpened after LLM review):** (i) **Pure-step** attention reallocation is disfavoured (it predicts no graded n-dependence), but a **GRADED** attention/bandwidth account is NOT formally excluded — a graded 'longer-context read' or a decode-time KV-buffer can also produce a monotone dose AND a difficulty gradient at flat baseline. The difficulty-selectivity is the stronger lever, but not decisive. (ii) **Localization is NOT established:** the data show filler ENABLES the benefit and that it scales with filler AMOUNT, but do NOT prove the computation happens AT the filler positions (vs at the decode step reading the filler positions as scratch/KV buffer) — so we say 'filler-enabled / filler-amount-dependent', NOT 'filler-time'. (iii) **Content vs length:** counting is structured/numeric; the dots probe (a different filler that also banks Q1 under not-told, Tier 3.5) shows it is not specific to counting's numeric content, but both are 'length' — a length-matched RANDOM filler would be needed to fully separate token-identity from positions-available. (iv) We do NOT claim 'parallel latent thinking' (disfavoured) and do NOT assert a proven cognitive mechanism. The decisive future tests are activation/logit-lens probing of the last filler position and a random-filler content control (flagged for the synthesis / future work).

# Segment 9 — DeepSeek in-structure B1(n) reference curve (k=1, minimal reveal, counting)

Level **add_n4_d10**, provider-pinned good cluster, parser=open, m_inst=4000.

k=1 n=0 in-structure baseline = **55.8%**. Paired bootstrap over instances (cluster=instance, seed 0). `n` = MEASURED input_tokens delta (counting ~2 tok/item).


## B1(n) curve (additive + Haldane-logit)

| items | realized tok | B1 additive (pp) [CI] | B1 logit [CI] |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | 11 | -1.1 [-2.7,+0.6] | -0.04 [-0.11,+0.02] |
| 12 | 23 | +4.0 [+2.3,+5.5] | +0.16 [+0.10,+0.23] |
| 18 | 35 | +4.1 [+2.5,+5.7] | +0.17 [+0.10,+0.23] |
| 25 | 49 | +7.7 [+6.1,+9.3] | +0.32 [+0.25,+0.39] |
| 38 | 75 | +6.9 [+5.3,+8.4] | +0.29 [+0.22,+0.35] |
| 50 | 99 | +6.7 [+5.1,+8.2] | +0.28 [+0.21,+0.34] |
| 75 | 149 | +7.1 [+5.6,+8.6] | +0.30 [+0.23,+0.36] |
| 100 | 199 | +8.2 [+6.6,+9.8] | +0.34 [+0.27,+0.41] |
| 150 | 299 | +4.6 [+2.9,+6.2] | +0.19 [+0.12,+0.25] |
| 200 | 399 | +8.7 [+7.1,+10.3] | +0.36 [+0.30,+0.43] |

## Interpolated references at the candidate operating points

Parallel = B1(n); divided(k) = B1(n/k). SE_R = bootstrap SE of the interpolated reference.

| operating n (tok) | parallel B1(n) | divided B1(n/2) | divided B1(n/4) | divided B1(n/8) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 100 | +6.7 (SE0.8) | +7.7 (SE0.8) [n/k=50] | +4.0 (SE0.8) [n/k=25] | -0.3 (SE0.8) [n/k=12] |
| 200 | +8.1 (SE0.8) | +6.7 (SE0.8) [n/k=100] | +7.7 (SE0.8) [n/k=50] | +4.0 (SE0.8) [n/k=25] |
| 400 | +8.7 (SE0.8) | +8.1 (SE0.8) [n/k=200] | +6.7 (SE0.8) [n/k=100] | +7.7 (SE0.8) [n/k=50] |

**n_lo (smallest grid tok with B1 CI>0) ≈ 23 tok.** 
DeepSeek's boost is modest (~+7-8pp plateau, +8.7pp at 400 tok) and saturates fast, so the parallel (B1(n)) and divided (B1(n/k)) references are CLOSE -> the parallel-vs-divided separation is weak (expected; lead with the directedness contrast). The divided-vs-null separation is usable where n/k stays on the plateau (e.g. k=8 @ n=400 tok: divided B1(50 tok)=+7.7pp, well above 0).

**Chosen per-k operating points (n/k >= ~50 tok, on the plateau):** k=2 -> n=200 tok, k=4 -> n=200 tok, k=8 -> n=400 tok (n=200 co-reported).

# Segment 9 — DeepSeek off-ceiling per-position check (n=0, reveal_after)

Level **add_n4_d10** (4x 10-digit addition — fast-saturating, the family that CEILINGED on Opus). Off-ceiling rule: reveal_after n=0 accuracy in [5%,85%]. Early pool = first ceil(k/2) positions (the clean read).


| k | per-position n=0 accuracy (label: acc, n) | early-pool labels | all off-ceiling? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | Q1*:53%(n3000) Q2:74%(n3000) | [1] | YES |
| 4 | Q1*:53%(n6000) Q2*:70%(n6000) Q3:74%(n6000) Q4:77%(n6000) | [1, 2] | YES |
| 8 | Q1*:53%(n6000) Q2*:70%(n6000) Q3*:75%(n6000) Q4*:72%(n6000) Q5:74%(n6000) Q6:75%(n6000) Q7:73%(n6000) Q8:75%(n6000) | [1, 2, 3, 4] | YES |

(* = early-pool position.)

## Verdict

- DeepSeek's **early/primacy positions stay OFF the ceiling** at k∈{2,4,8} (CONFIRMED): Q1 (the primacy-disadvantaged, cleanest read) is ~52-56% across k, well within [5%,85%]. Later positions run higher (~70-80% — the questions-as-filler preceding-context effect) but stay below the 85% ceiling.
- So **add_n4_d10 is ADOPTED** as the DeepSeek off-ceiling mid-band level (no need for a more gradual/harder level): the position-resolved early-pool read has headroom for both the reveal_after B_k and the directedness contrast. (The aggregate n=0 baseline RISES with k — the SAME questions-as-filler / recency phenomenology documented on Opus — which is why we read position-resolved, not the aggregate.)

# Instructions

## Project context and motivation (read carefully — this is the FINAL segment)

You are completing a multi-segment empirical research project. The full plan is in `OVERALL_PLAN.md`
in your working directory, and the prior work is documented in the **unified** `writeups/write_up.md`
(read it end-to-end — especially the Status block, the ⚑ verdict box, and the per-segment "Segment
0…11" sections) plus the phase-specific `writeups/write_up_segment_{8,9,10,11}_phase_0.md` and

must hold the whole arc in your head before you start.

**The research question (the whole project).** When a recent LLM answers a math problem with **no
chain-of-thought** (the answer is literally the first token it emits), it gets one forward pass of
compute at the answer position. Padding the prompt with semantically-empty **filler tokens** (counting
`1 2 3 …`) *before* the answer raises no-CoT math accuracy — the extra positions give the model
somewhere to do hidden computation (a per-question boost `B₁(n)`). **The core question:** if the model
is given *k* difficulty-matched questions that **share the same total *n* filler**, then one is revealed
**by index after the filler** and only that single answer is read, does each question keep the full
boost (**parallel latent thinking**, `B_k≈B₁(n)`), does the boost shrink as questions share a fixed pool
(**divided pool**, `B_k≈B₁(n/k)`), or does the model **not bank computation it can't direct** (**null**,
`B_k≈0`)? The pre-specified headline statistic was a **sharing exponent α** (`n_eff = n/k^α`; α=0
parallel, α=1 divided); the co-primary disambiguator is the **reveal-before vs reveal-after directedness
contrast**.

**The project is scientifically COMPLETE. This segment does NOT run new experiments.** Your job is to
(1) **consolidate everything into a clean, coherent, honest final write-up** that a fresh reader can
understand, leading with the evolved headline; (2) **polish and verify the headline figures**; (3)
**verify reproducibility** (`--assert-cached`); and (4) **run the full review battery and FIX what it

## ⚑ The final headline — what the project found (state THIS, in this shape)

Across Segments 0–11 the answer to the core question converged on a **framing-robust anti-parallel
result with framing-conditional refinements and a characterized mechanism.** The synthesis must present
it as a **clean convergent result, NOT a sequence of reversals** (the headline genuinely sharpened over
the project; tell it as a sharpening, with the pre-registered verdict honored and the later
scope-widening labeled exploratory). The robust core and its refinements:

- **The robust core (framing-INDEPENDENT): the model does NOT do parallel latent thinking across the k
  shared questions.** It banks **at most ~one question's worth** of filler computation, committed to a
  **single** question — it never spreads compute across the k. So the per-question *undirected* boost
  stays **`≪ the divided-pool prediction` at every k∈{2,3,4,6,8,16}** (Segments 7/8/10) and far below
  the parallel reference, on **counting AND dots** filler, and the picture **partially replicates on the
  open model DeepSeek** (the directedness + undirected-declines-with-sharing + ≪-divided *structure*
  generalizes; weaker magnitude; cross-model is confounded with cross-level).
- **The pre-registered Segment-8 verdict (honored, scoped):** under the pre-registered **encouraging
  ("told") framing**, at the most-shared k=8, the undirected reveal-after `B_k` is **NEAR-NULL** (NULL on
  the cleaner Q1-only/held-out estimands, statistically PARTIAL/sub-divided +2.5pp on the pooled
  early-average; ~7% of B₁; ≪ divided at ~10σ), while the **directedness contrast is robustly POSITIVE
  (+16.2pp)** → combined **"partial directed."** A **retention gradient** (less-shared cells retain
  more: 23%@k=2 → 7%@k=8). **α is NOT estimated** (positivity gate fails — ill-defined near the null,
  the pre-registered deviation). This is the formal, frozen-rules result on the pre-registered grid.
- **WHERE the single-question budget lands depends on directedness + framing (Segment 10 V1, exploratory
  scope-widening — a major finding):** **directed** (reveal-before, target named before the filler) → the
  named question gets a large, framing-robust boost (the confound-minimal `reveal_before − disclose`
  contrast is ~+24pp). **Undirected + "told"** (the prompt **discloses** "one of these, chosen at random,
  will be asked") → the commitment is **suppressed** → the near-null Segment-8 result. **Undirected +
  "not-told"** (no disclosure) → the model commits **~one question's worth to its DEFAULT first-listed
  Q1** (k=8 Q1 boost +26.3pp; n-scaling). **The driver is the random-target DISCLOSURE, not the
  encouragement** (pinned by the `disclose` arm: disclose ≈ told ≪ not-told). So "the model does not
  bank UNDIRECTED computation" is *refuted under not-told* — but **the anti-parallel core SURVIVES**
  (under neither framing does it bank >~1 question's worth).
- **Mechanism (Segment 11, exploratory; cross-family + cross-model confirmed in DIRECTION):** the filler
  benefit is a **genuine, dose-responsive, difficulty-selective, filler-amount-dependent** boost
  committed to ONE question (the named target — target-following but **primacy-modulated**; or, absent
  disclosure, the **positional default Q1**). The default target is **positional**, not content-based.
  The in-prompt directed boost is **capped at ~half the lone boost** by the multi-question structure
  (most of the cap appears at the first distractor; partly questions-as-filler headroom pre-consumption).
- **Honest interpretation caution (do NOT overclaim — this is load-bearing):** the regime/directedness
  verdict is robust to mechanism, but the stronger claim "the model does k questions' worth of latent
  thinking in parallel" is **disfavored**, not merely unproven. The genuine-hidden-computation reading is
  **STRENGTHENED but NOT PROVEN**: a *pure-step* attention/recency-reallocation account is disfavored (no
  graded n-dependence), but a *graded* attention/bandwidth account is **not formally excluded**;
  **localization is not established** (hence "filler-amount-dependent," NOT "filler-time"); and the
  cleanest genuine-computation evidence is **Opus-sumprod-leaning**. State what is robustly shown vs what
  remains suggestive.

# Overall Plan: Filler Tokens — Parallel Latent Thinking?

## 1. Core objective

When a recent LLM answers a math problem with **no chain-of-thought** (the answer is the
first thing it emits), it gets only one forward pass of compute at the answer position.
Padding the prompt with semantically-empty "filler" tokens (e.g. `1 2 3 ... 300`) before the
answer measurably raises no-CoT math accuracy — the extra positions appear to give the model
somewhere to do hidden computation ([Redwood, 2026](https://blog.redwoodresearch.org/p/recent-llms-can-use-filler-tokens);
[Pfau et al., 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.15758)).

**The research question:** can the model spread that hidden computation across *several
questions at once*? Give it one question + *n* filler tokens → some accuracy boost
`B₁(n) = acc(1 question, n filler) − acc(no filler)`. Now give it *k* difficulty-matched
questions sharing the *same total n* filler, and measure the per-question boost `B_k(n)`. Two
clean regimes (with a continuum and a third null in between — see below):

- **Parallel latent thinking:** `B_k(n) ≈ B₁(n)` — the boost doesn't care how many questions
  share the filler.
- **Shared pool (no free lunch):** `B_k(n) ≈ B₁(n/k)` — filler is one compute pool divided
  across questions.

**Pre-specified headline statistic — the sharing exponent α.** Rather than only checking the two
extremes, fit an *effective filler* `n_eff = n / k^α` such that `B_k(n) ≈ B₁(n_eff)` across all
*k*, *n*. Then **α = 0 ⇒ fully parallel, α = 1 ⇒ fully divided**, and intermediate α ⇒ partial
sharing. Report α with a bootstrap CI (resampling over *questions*, respecting the paired design).
This is the primary analysis; the boost-vs-*n* / boost-vs-(*n/k*) collapse plots are the visual
version (which x-axis collapses the lines: *n* ⇒ parallel, *n/k* ⇒ divided). If a single intuitive
number helps, also report the fraction of the single-question boost retained when *k* share the
filler.

**A third, very plausible outcome to name explicitly:** `B_k(n) ≈ 0` for *k* > 1 in the
reveal-after design — the model simply does not bank computation it can't direct. This is distinct
from "divided pool" and is disambiguated by the reveal-before control (§2). The deliverable also
degrades gracefully: if the boost is too small / saturates too fast to power the comparison, ship a
**clean replication + the `B₁(n)` curve + a characterization of what task would resolve it**.

**Interpretation caution (carry into the write-up):** the collapse/α test cleanly adjudicates
*parallel vs divided sharing of the filler benefit*, but `B_k ≈ B₁` is *also* consistent with
"filler gives a generic, per-question-independent benefit for reasons unrelated to genuine parallel
hidden computation." The regime verdict is robust to mechanism; the stronger claim "the model does
k questions' worth of latent thinking in parallel" is only suggestively supported. State both.

## 2. Why the design choices matter (read before planning any phase)

These are load-bearing and several are easy to get subtly wrong:

- **Genuine no-CoT is the whole premise.** The answer must be literally the first content
  token the model emits. If the model sneaks in any reasoning, the "one forward pass" framing
  collapses. We enforce this with assistant **prefill** (the prompt is seeded so the next generated
  token is the answer) and verify by inspecting raw outputs. Extended thinking / reasoning is **off**.
- **Procedural arithmetic (not a fixed benchmark)** is the primary task, to avoid memorization
  confounds and to give a *difficulty knob* for (a) placing baseline accuracy in a measurable
  mid-band and (b) generating difficulty-matched questions for the *k*-question prompts. A
  competition-math-style set serves as a secondary anchor / **positive control** for the harness.
- **Filler must demonstrably help on our task.** Pfau et al. find filler helps *parallelizable*
  computation most; not every arithmetic task will show a boost. Finding a procedural task where
  filler reliably and largely boosts no-CoT accuracy is a core early de-risk, not a given.
- **The single-random-question control is essential.** If the model emits answers in order, later
  answers get earlier ones as free compute and `B_k` looks parallel by *artifact*. So each prompt
  presents *k* questions, then the filler, then reveals **one randomly chosen** question to answer,
  and we read only that single answer.
- **The reveal is by INDEX/LABEL, not by restatement.** Label the questions (Q1…Q*k*) before the
  filler; after the filler, ask "answer Q3" *without* re-printing Q3's text. Restating the full
  question after the filler is a design trap: it places a fresh, salient copy of the target adjacent
  to the answer position and confounds the comparison with `B₁`, potentially letting the model lean
  less on filler-position computation. Reveal-by-index keeps the question text in a fixed position
  (before the filler) so the experiment tests what we intend. (The questions remain in context, so
  "recall which is Q3" is trivial; verify this in the pilot.)
- **Reveal-after vs reveal-before — both are primary.** In reveal-**after**, the target is unknown
  during the filler (the parallel-thinking test). But note `B_k < B₁` then has *two* explanations:
  (i) shared pool, or (ii) the model can't use filler effectively when it doesn't know which
  question to compute (an *undirectedness* penalty), independent of any pool. The **reveal-before**
  condition (same *k* questions, but the target is revealed *before* the filler, with *k*−1
  distractors present) isolates directedness from context interference and is needed to interpret
  the headline. The clean condition set for the main experiment:
  - **reveal-after, k=1** (target known, no distractors) — the structure-matched `B₁(n)`.
  - **reveal-after, k>1** (target unknown, distractors) — the main `B_k(n)`.
  - **reveal-before, k>1** (target known, distractors) — isolates the undirectedness penalty.

  reveal-before vs reveal-after at fixed *k* ⇒ directedness; reveal-before(*k*) vs
  reveal-after(*k*=1) ⇒ pure context interference.
- **Encouraging framing is the default, not an ablation.** Whether the model *attempts* all *k*
  during the filler likely depends on telling it "you will be asked to answer exactly one of these,
  so be ready for any." Make this the main design; "not told" is the ablation.
- **Read the rise, not the height.** More questions can lower the *n*=0 baseline via plain context
  interference, which is *not* what we care about. Baseline each *k* at its own *n*=0 and compare the
  *shapes/rises*. Because additive accuracy boosts can mean different things at different baselines
  (floor/ceiling, regression), do the collapse/α analysis on the raw-accuracy boost **and** on a
  logit scale as a robustness check, and verify baselines aren't so divergent that additive
  comparison is invalid.
- **Statistical power is the central practical risk** (see §3). The whole question is moot unless
  `B₁(n)` is large and rising over a wide enough *n*-range that the regimes are separable.

## 3. Risks and likely failure modes

1. **Replication fails / filler boost doesn't reproduce on our harness+task.** Mitigate by trying
   multiple filler types (counting, dots, repeated problem), filler placements (user prompt vs
   assistant prefill), several procedural task families, and the strongest reported model (Opus 4.5)
   first — **and by validating the harness against a known-positive competition-math set so a null
   on procedural arithmetic is interpretable (harness-broken vs task-doesn't-show-it).** De-risk in
   Phase 0.
2. **No-CoT not genuinely enforced** (model reasons anyway, or answer extraction is wrong). Mitigate
   with prefill + small-but-non-truncating max_tokens + strict answer format + manual output
   inspection. Verify Opus 4.5 supports prefill (it predates 4.6/4.7/4.8 which don't; earlier
   Anthropic models do).
3. **Boost saturates too fast or is too small → no statistical power.** *The* key risk. The `B₁(n)`
   segment finds a task + operating point where the boost is large and still rising over a wide
   *n*-range. The **n-grid must keep `n / k_max` inside B₁'s informative (rising) region** — if
   `n/8` falls below the threshold where filler starts helping, the divided prediction flattens to
   ≈0 and the regimes converge except at the extreme, killing discriminating power. If unresolvable
   after real effort (including the conditional harder-task-search segment), ship graceful
   degradation.
4. **Insufficient samples to separate the regimes.** `B₁` may be only a few points; the gap between
   regimes can be smaller. Need large samples per cell (cheap: no-CoT output is a few tokens; Opus
   input is the cost driver; open models are ~free). Do an explicit power analysis on pilot data
   before the full run; pre-register the α analysis to avoid garden-of-forking-paths across the
   k×n×condition×model cells.
5. **n=0 interference confound** (larger *k* lowers the baseline for reasons unrelated to filler
   sharing). Handled by analyzing rises / collapse plots, plus the reveal-before control and the
   logit-scale check.
6. **Directedness confound** (reveal-after k>1 deficits could be undirectedness, not pooling).
   Handled by the reveal-before co-primary condition.
7. **Open-model complications:** the effect may not reproduce on a given open model; reasoning modes
   must be disabled; prefill support varies. Dedicated segment; try several. **Note:** design/scoring/
   prompt-format decisions transfer from the open model to Opus, but the *operating point, n-grid,
   and effect magnitude are model-specific* and must be set on the headline model (Opus 4.5).
8. **Difficulty mismatch** between B₁ questions and the per-question difficulty inside k-prompts
   would invalidate the comparison. Procedural generation at a fixed difficulty handles this; verify.

## 4. Models and infrastructure

- **Primary (headline) model: Opus 4.5** (`claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) — strongest reported effect,
  and it supports assistant prefill (needed for guaranteed no-CoT).
- **Open model: TBD via a selection segment** — candidates on OpenRouter (`qwen3-235b-a22b`,
  `qwen3-32b`, `deepseek-v3.2`, `kimi-k2`, …). No local GPU and vLLM not installed, so default to
  OpenRouter (or Tinker for inference) rather than self-hosting. Open models on OpenRouter typically
  support prefill and run cheaply at scale (good for dense sweeps, pilots, and a cross-model check).
- **API keys are environment variables** in this sandbox (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`,
  `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `TINKER_API_KEY`), already set and also in `/workspace/.env`. There are **no**
  `~/.anthropic_api_key` files — code must use the env vars (SDKs read them automatically; the
  `calling-llm-apis` skill says keys live at `~/.x_api_key`, which is wrong for this sandbox).
- Measure filler size in **tokens via the Anthropic API `input_tokens` delta** (with vs without
  filler) for Opus; tiktoken is only a rough cross-check (wrong tokenizer for Anthropic).
- Standard infra: `file_cache.py`, `cost_tracker.py`, async + semaphores, JSONL streaming results,
  fixed seeds, git hash in metadata, `--assert-cached` reproducibility.
- **Cost:** affordable well within the default $5k budget (no-CoT output is tiny; Opus input
  dominates; open models cheap). Track in `total_cost.jsonl`. Wall-clock/rate limits matter more —
  lean on concurrency and caching.

## 5. Segment breakdown

Logical chain: **trust the measurement → characterize the single-question boost → run the main
experiment (with its directedness control) → make it robust and write it up.** Finish each segment
cleanly before moving on; the boost must reproduce before anything downstream is meaningful.

- **Segment 0 — Harness + procedural arithmetic + replication de-risk + positive control (Opus 4.5).**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).** Lead with a minimal manual smoke test (confirm prefill works and the
  effect direction on a few hand-made problems). Then build project infra and the core harness: cached
  Opus 4.5 calls, prefill-based genuine no-CoT + answer extraction/scoring, filler insertion, and a
  first-pass difficulty-tunable procedural arithmetic generator. Run a small-scale paired
  no-filler-vs-filler de-risk **plus a known-positive competition-math anchor as a harness positive
  control**, so a null is interpretable. Verify genuine no-CoT by inspecting raw outputs. **The
  critical first de-risk.** (Not: large sweeps, open model, dataset-validation pipeline, the main
  experiment.)
  - **Outcome:** the filler boost **reproduces strongly and selectively** on Opus 4.5. Paired n=500
    boosts: addition +17 to +30 pp, mul +11, GSM-Hard (positive control) +13, all p≈0, robust on a
    logit scale. Genuine no-CoT verified (prefill; 0% text-before-number on arithmetic; no
    truncation). **Selectivity:** filler helps **one deep multi-digit arithmetic computation**, not
    "aggregate many easy checks" (count_correct null even at matched ~48% baseline) — refines the
    Pfau parallelizable-computation framing and argues against a generic format artifact. The phase
    also (beyond scope) began the `B₁(n)` finer sweep and ran 3 review rounds with real fixes.
  - **Two carry-forward facts for downstream:** (1) the boost **saturates early / the rising region
    is narrow** (most of the boost by ~10–150 filler tokens; no degradation to ~1000) — a real power
    risk for the k-question experiment (the Segment 2 gate). (2) The harness is validated for an
    *arithmetic-precision* boost; we never got a clean reasoning/competition-math positive (AIME 2025
    is contaminated/underpowered → demoted). A decontaminated post-cutoff competition set is a
    later-segment item.

- **Segment 1 — Procedural arithmetic dataset(s) + difficulty calibration.**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).** Used `dataset-creation` (desiderata first) to finalize, validate, and
  **save canonical datasets to `data/`** on top of the Segment-0 generator.
  - **Outcome:** shipped **6 canonical levels across 3 deep-arithmetic families** (5000 deterministic
    items each; full metadata + a deterministic `canonical.load_canonical` loader): four multi-operand
    **addition** levels spanning the mid-band (`add_n16_d8`≈47%, **primary** `add_n15_d8`≈55%,
    `add_n12_d8`≈68% k-headroom, `add_n15_d6`≈52% / +30pp max-boost), a **homogenized multiplication**
    level (`mul_a4_b4_hom`≈47%), and an all-positive **sum-of-products** level (`sumprod_t3_d2`≈40%).
    Baselines are pooled n=1000 (calib+held-out) with bootstrap CIs; every level passes a
    filler-responsiveness gate (+11 to +30 pp, counting AND dots). Full validation PASSES
    (determinism/prefix-stability, exact big-int recompute, uniqueness + canonical-form dedup,
    negligible digit-pattern artifacts, scoring consistent on 24k+ cached outputs, difficulty
    homogeneity with no degenerate cliffs). Desiderata LLM-critiqued; `dataset-reviewer` + several
    research/data-inspector review rounds with real fixes. Scope held (no `B₁(n)` curve, k-experiment,
    or open model).
  - **Carry-forward facts:** (1) for addition, **#operands at fixed 8-digit operands is the clean
    monotonic difficulty knob**; 6-digit is non-smooth (a confirmed localized `add_n14_d6` dip; the
    shipped d6 level is fine). (2) Under filler, **mul/sumprod emit ~7% spaced-digit outputs** → use
    `harness.parse_answer_robust` when measuring their boost magnitude; addition stays clean. (3)
    **Selection-bias guard:** the default loader slice `[0:n]` is the calibration sample — downstream
    *fresh* draws must pass `offset>=500` (calib=[0,500), held-out=[3000,3500)). (4) The three d8
    addition levels are **nested prefixes** (correlated across difficulty). (5) sum-of-products answers
    are 4–5 digit vs ~9–10 for addition — a cross-family magnitude caveat (constant within a family).

- **Segment 2 — `B₁(n)` single-question boost curve (THE MAKE-OR-BREAK POWER GATE).**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). VERDICT: GO.** Measured the fine-grained paired `B₁(n)` boost on Opus 4.5
  over a 19-point realized-token grid (0–1200 tok) on **fresh** canonical items (`offset≥500`),
  m=1000/cell, bootstrap CIs over items; robust parser for mul/sumprod. Folded in a consolidated
  fresh-item replication figure (so the old standalone replication segment is fully retired).
  - **Outcome:** the make-or-break gate **PASSES**. The narrow-rising-region risk does **not** block
    the experiment because the boost **plateau is wide** (≈flat, no degradation to ≥1200 tok for
    addition): operate total filler *n* on the plateau while `n/k` sits just above the helping
    threshold `n_lo`, giving `Δ = B₁(n) − B₁(n/k) ≈ plateau − threshold`. The parallel (`B_k≈B₁(n)`)
    vs divided (`B_k≈B₁(n/k)`) references are **separable for the full planned k∈{2,4,8}** on multiple
    levels (m=1000 counting separations: `add_n15_d6` 8.8σ, `sumprod_t3_d2` 6.7σ, `add_n12_d8` 6.1σ;
    `add_n15_d8` marginal 3.6σ; `mul_a4_b4_hom` is **NO-GO** — small Δ + degrades). Rising region fully
    characterized (8-digit addition saturates ~7 tok; `add_n15_d6` dips −8pp at ≤11 tok then steps up
    at ~29 tok; sumprod gradual). Harder-difficulty probe: naively harder addition does **NOT** widen
    the rising region — it just hits the floor; the wide plateau is tied to **boost magnitude / digit
    structure**, not raw difficulty.
  - **Carry-forward for the main-experiment design/lock-in:** (1) the headline statistic is the
    continuous **α-fit + 3-way (parallel/divided/`B_k≈0` null) verdict**, which needs a **GRADED rise**
    so the divided prediction spans intermediate values (a steep step pins divided ≈threshold, barely
    separating divided-from-null). Recommended: run **≥2 levels** — a graded-rise headline primary
    (**`add_n15_d6` with DOTS filler**: clean addition, wide +27pp plateau, graded div-slope ~0.3,
    k=4 7.9σ / k=8 8.7σ, but k=2 marginal 2.4σ; or **`sumprod_t3_d2`**) plus a max-signal confirmatory
    level (**`add_n15_d6` counting** / **`add_n12_d8`**); `add_n15_d10` is a clean-addition gradual
    alternative. (2) **Filler type is a design lever** (counting → steep step; dots → graded rise on
    d6). Candidate n-grid ≈ {50,100,200,400} tok × k∈{2,4,8}, designed **per-level AND per-filler-type**
    so each k has a graded cell with `n/k ≥ n_lo` (≈25 tok for d6); keep `n/k ≥ n_lo` so the divided
    prediction isn't a *negative* (dip) or sub-threshold value. (3) the divided prediction `B₁(n/k)` is
    an **abstraction** read off the single-question curve — the gate proves the two references are
    separable, not where real `B_k` lands (the `B_k≈0` null is still possible); Δ is baseline-cancelling
    but the k-prompt has its own lower n=0 baseline → per-k baselining + logit checks still needed.
    (4) **dots-in-the-k-prompt is unvalidated** (validate in the design pilot). Cost ≈$302 cumulative;
    the full curve reproduces via `--assert-cached` at $0.

- **Segment 3 — Open-model selection + replication.** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). SELECTED:
  DeepSeek-V3-0324.** Built a cached OpenRouter wrapper (`llm_openrouter.py`) + a **model-agnostic
  harness** (`harness.answer_problem(..., caller=...)`, defaults to Opus → byte-identical cache-hits
  for all prior Opus runs); surveyed **9 recent open models**.
  - **Outcome:** **DeepSeek-V3-0324** (`deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324`) selected — the only surveyed
    open model with both **cleanly enforceable genuine no-CoT** (reasoning OFF via
    `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":False}}`; prefill honored; 0% reasoning field, 0.026%
    letters-before-digit, 0.01% truncation, median 6 out-tokens) **and** a clean reproducible filler
    boost. At a **per-model** mid-band level **`add_n4_d10`** (4× 10-digit addition, base 52%; Opus
    canonical levels are at the FLOOR for it — 0% on `add_n15_d8`), the boost is **modest but genuine:
    counting +5.2pp [2.3,8.0], dots +5.7pp [3.1,8.3]** (logit-robust, CI excludes 0), counting ≈ dots,
    with a usable dip-then-rise dynamic range (small-n dip then plateau by ~100–200 tok, no degradation
    to ~600 tok). Genuine computation (0% give-ups; level-specific — null at `add_n4_d12`/`add_n5_d10`/
    `add_n5_d8`; carry-near-miss error structure). Realized-token mapping re-measured for DeepSeek
    (dots ≈1 tok/item, counting ≈2 tok/item — NOT the Anthropic mapping).
  - **MAJOR methodological finding (transfers):** unpinned OpenRouter routing across heterogeneous
    provider **quantizations** inflated the first-pass boost ~2× (+11→+5.7pp) via a size-dependent mix
    (a low-quant cluster {AtlasCloud/Novita/GMICloud ≈22–29%} served more n=0 than filler calls,
    depressing the baseline). **Provider pinning/allowlisting a homogeneous cluster** {DeepInfra,
    ModelRun, SiliconFlow ≈54–56%} is **MANDATORY** for any OpenRouter open-model arithmetic eval;
    confirmed clean by a powered single-DeepInfra run (dots +5.0 [2.6,7.3], counting +3.9 [1.0,6.7]).
    Caught + fixed across **3 review rounds / 5 review subagents.**
  - **Carry-forward facts:** (1) the open-model boost is **modest (~+4–6pp) and narrow** (clear only at
    `add_n4_d10`) — fine for the cheap **design-pilot** platform (mechanics/format transfer), but the
    cross-model main experiment (Segment 9) must set its own operating point, **pin a provider**, run
    large samples, and **may find a tighter/absent parallel-divided separation** than Opus (its own
    power gate). (2) **dots-as-a-lever is Opus-specific** — on DeepSeek counting ≈ dots in both
    magnitude and rise shape. (3) Dots filler occasionally makes DeepSeek emit a benign "answer hidden
    in the dots" joke before the integer (≤0.03%; still parses) — watch this inside k>1 prompts. (4) Most
    recent strong open models DON'T replicate the boost with genuine no-CoT (Qwen3-235B genuinely *hurts*
    at clean no-CoT; DeepSeek-V3.2/gpt-oss-120b/llama-3.3 disqualified on no-CoT enforceability) — a
    real cross-model finding. **Whether to seek a stronger/second open model (or use a 2nd strong
    *closed* model for the cross-model arm) is a Segment-9 decision**, not a gap here.

- **Segment 4 — Main-experiment design + pilot.** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0).** Built and validated the
  **k-question reveal-by-index apparatus** (`kharness.py`/`run_kquestion.py`/`kanalysis.py`,
  model-agnostic): single-random-reveal read-one-answer; reveal-after AND reveal-before; encouraging
  framing default + framing knob; difficulty-matched i.i.d. *k* items; a 4-turn chat (questions →
  filler assistant turn → reveal-by-index → answer prefill; n=0 = empty filler turn).
  - **Mechanics all validated** (DeepSeek-heavy + Opus confirm; `results/inspect_kquestion_deepseek.md`):
    genuine no-CoT holds in the k-structure (≤0.02% violations, 0% reasoning field, ~0 truncation); the
    model answers the **asked** question (100% of matched ints; 0% match a distractor); index→question
    recall is trivial (the +9–12pp restate gap is recency/salience, not recall); scoring clean (0
    conservative-vs-robust disagreements across ~56k outputs).
  - **⚑ TWO load-bearing findings that OVERTURN planning assumptions** (the reason a new Segment 5 was
    inserted): **(1) the n=0 baseline RISES with k (it does NOT drop)** — distractor questions act as
    *filler* for one another (a position effect averaged over the random reveal: positions ≥2 get a
    preceding-question "filler" boost that *saturates* after ~1 preceding question; **Q1 is strongly
    primacy-disadvantaged** and degrades with k). So **fast addition (d8/d12) CEILINGS at high k**
    (additive comparison invalid; Opus add_n12_d8 k=8=96%, positions 2–8 ≈100%), while gradual-rise
    `sumprod_t3_d2` stays **mid-band across k (34%) and across positions (18–56%, none ceilinged)** — the
    desirable profile. **(2) a VERBOSE reveal turn SUPPRESSES the boost** (d6+dots → ≈0), but the
    **adopted MINIMAL reveal** (`"Now answer Q3:"`) RECOVERS it to ~single-turn on every level/filler
    (incl. d6+dots — NOT dead). The worker hit the task-2a "STOP and rework" gate and FIXED the apparatus.
  - **⚑ The Segment-2 single-turn `B₁(n)` curves do NOT transfer** — the **in-structure** (multi-turn,
    minimal-reveal) curve is **NARROWER** (sumprod peaks +28–36 @~200 tok then declines, vs the
    single-turn +23 flat plateau to 801 tok). A full **in-structure `B₁(n)` re-measurement is a HARD
    prerequisite** for operating-point selection (now Segment 5).
  - **⚑ Preliminary pilot `B_k`: reveal-after k>1 ≈ 0/negative on both models** (sumprod k=4/8 −6/−1pp;
    DeepSeek similar), while **reveal-before (directed) shows a clear boost** (sumprod minimal k=4 before
    +16.5 vs after ≈0) — i.e. the **`B_k≈0` null** ("the model doesn't bank undirected computation") is a
    *live, plausible headline*. BUT this is **partly a pre-saturation confound, not purely the science**:
    a position-resolved B_k (sumprod k=8) shows filler helps primacy Q1 ~0 but **HURTS the pre-saturated
    late positions** (Q6–8 −16 to −28pp). The **reveal-before vs reveal-after contrast** (same
    pre-saturation in both) is the clean disambiguator and is now elevated to a primary interpretive tool.
    **Variance for sizing:** paired-boost SE ≈ 3.3pp@n=250, 2.0pp@n=600 → ~1500–2000 instances/cell to
    resolve a ~2pp between-regime Δ.
  - **Carry-forward:** prefer a **gradual-rise level that stays mid-band across k AND keeps positions off
    the ceiling** (`sumprod_t3_d2` leads; `add_n15_d6`+counting/dots and the non-canonical `add_n15_d10`
    are alternatives; d8/d12 ceiling). Use **counting** on sumprod (dots triggers a ~1% show-work).
    **Balance revealed positions**; analyze on the **logit scale** (baseline shifts a lot with k).
    Consider a **revealed-item-fixed (paired-across-k) design** to control revealed-item difficulty +
    position exactly and cut sample needs.

- **Segment 5 — In-structure `B₁(n)` re-measurement + pre-saturation/position confound investigation +
  experiment-design finalization + power-gate re-confirmation.** **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). VERDICT: GO
  for k∈{4,8}, PARTIAL for k=2.** Re-measured the in-structure `B₁(n)` curve (multi-turn, **minimal
  reveal**, k=1, m=1000), characterized the pre-saturation/position confound, re-confirmed the gate
  in-structure (3-way), and finalized + validated the confound-aware design.
  - **Outcome:** (1) the Segment-4 "in-structure is NARROWER" claim was a **full-reveal artifact** —
    on matched items the minimal-reveal in-structure boost is only ~+5pp above single-turn and the n=0
    baseline is identical, an offset that **CANCELS in the gate Δ and the contrast**; the real shape
    feature is that **`sumprod_t3_d2` PEAKS +37pp @150–200 tok then DECLINES** (operate near the peak).
    (2) The pre-saturation confound: distractor questions act as preceding-context filler, so
    **FAST-saturating levels (`add_n15_d10`, `add_n12_d8`) push late positions to the CEILING → their
    aggregate `B_k≈0` is a no-headroom artifact**; **only the gradual `sumprod_t3_d2` keeps every k=8
    position off the ceiling (21–52%)** → it is the headline level (`add_n15_d6` is off-ceiling but
    carry-fragile → secondary; `add_n15_d10` REJECTED, not saved). Explicit filler **erases the recency
    advantage** of late positions, so the position-balanced aggregate `B_k=−6.8pp` is a recency-erasure
    mixture → analyze **position-resolved** (early/unconfounded positions = the clean read). (3) The
    in-structure 3-way gate is GO for k∈{4,8} (Δ par−div +11.8/+22.3; Δ divided−null +25.3/+14.7 at
    n=200), PARTIAL for k=2 (recovers at n=100); the **binding constraint Δ divided−null is large because
    sumprod's gradual rise keeps divided well above 0**. **Context interference caps any positive B_k** —
    even DIRECTED a k-prompt question gets ~+12–18pp vs the lone +37, so the **parallel reference is
    essentially unreachable** and the gate's real value is the divided-vs-null resolution + the contrast.
    (4) The **likely headline is the `B_k≈0` undirected null** (the model uses filler when it knows the
    target but does not bank undirected computation), carried by a large cross-level/cross-model
    **reveal-before/after directedness contrast** (Opus sumprod +18.9pp k=8; `add_n15_d6` +32.8pp;
    DeepSeek +8–11pp); position-resolved early-position reveal-after B_k = +2.8pp [+1.2,+4.5] EXCLUDES
    both parallel (+37) and divided (+14.7).
  - **Finalized design:** `sumprod_t3_d2`+counting (PRIMARY, off-ceiling) + `add_n15_d6`+counting
    (SECONDARY consistency); position-BALANCE + position-RESOLVED; **reveal-before/after contrast =
    primary interpretive tool** (with n=0 control); additive **AND** logit scales; a revealed-item-fixed
    design implemented + validated but **REJECTED as primary** (corr(B_k,B1)≈0 → no variance gain; kept
    as an exact-control arm). Validated by Opus + DeepSeek pilots; 3 review rounds with real fixes.
  - **⚑ Headline framing EVOLVED (load-bearing for Segment 6):** because the likely outcome is the
    `B_k≈0` null, the pre-specified **α-fit is ILL-DEFINED at the null** → the **PRIMARY headline
    statistic is the 3-way resolution + the directedness contrast, read position-resolved**; the
    **α-fit/collapse plots are SECONDARY** (only where B_k is meaningfully positive). This is an
    enrichment of the *same* `B_k(n)` data (driven by the pre-saturation discovery), to be **explicitly
    pre-registered** as a deviation. **Sizing must use position-resolved variance** (~4–8× the aggregate
    m; per-position SE ≈1.7pp@n=500), and the **n-grid uses per-k operating points** (n=100 for k=2,
    n=200 for k=4,8) respecting `n/k ≥ n_lo` (sumprod n_lo≈12–24 tok, dip at ~6 tok).

- **Segment 6 — Power analysis + operating-point lock-in + analysis pre-registration.**
  **STATUS: DONE (Phase 0). The experiment plan is LOCKED + pre-registered (`results/preregistration.md`);
  a confirmation pilot passed all four validity guards.**
  - **Outcome:** sized per-cell on **position-resolved** variance (locked **m_inst = 3000/2000/1000/1000**
    for k=1/2/4/8; cluster bootstrap over drawn instances → early-pool SE ≈ 1.18/1.12/0.61pp; every primary
    cell excludes the divided reference by ≥10σ and the contrast by ~5–15σ; **total Segment-7 ≈ 272k Opus
    calls ≈ $697 gross**, much already cached). Locked grid: `sumprod_t3_d2`+counting (PRIMARY, off-ceiling)
    + `add_n15_d6`+counting (SECONDARY); k∈{1,2,4,8}; **HYBRID** per-k n-grid (operating points n=100 for
    k=2, n=200 for k=4,8, all `n/k ≥ 25 tok`, near the +37 peak, ≤400 to avoid the decline) + shared n/k
    anchors (25/50/100) for the conditional collapse plots; reveal-after AND reveal-before each with n=0;
    `sweep_positions` (balanced) analyzed **position-resolved** (early pool = first ⌈k/2⌉ positions, Q1-only
    co-reported); revealed-item-fixed as a robustness arm. A bounded 2nd-off-ceiling-level probe correctly
    added none (harder sumprod variants floor; `sumprod_t2_d3` is off-ceiling but floor-adjacent — documented
    for later). **Confirmation pilot (incl. a FRESH Opus k=4,8 @ n=200 confirm beyond the Segment-5 design
    max → de-circularized) PASSED all guards** (reveal-before positive control sig.+ on fresh draws +16.3
    [+13.2,+19.4]; off-ceiling 18–56%; no-CoT clean genuine-violation ≤0.08%; variance matches). The
    **pre-registration** is comprehensive and unusually self-critical (7 review-driven versions; 2 LLM rounds
    + `research-reviewer` + `empirical-ml-data-inspector`): both frames with the parallel-unreachable +
    interference-naive-divided caveats; PRIMARY = position-resolved 3-way + directedness contrast (additive
    AND logit) with mutually-exclusive TOST-style CI decision rules + a single headline-declaration template;
    SECONDARY = α only where B_k>0; the exact grid/m/budget; a small Bonferroni-corrected confirmatory family
    {sumprod k=8 regime, sumprod k=8 contrast, d6 k=8 contrast}; a pre-registered quantitative point
    prediction; a fresh-only firewall; hard validity guards; graceful degradation; and an explicit
    deviation-from-α note. **Likely headline (already visible in the pilot): NEAR-NULL / WEAK-POSITIVE
    PARTIAL + DIRECTEDNESS-POSITIVE** — headline cell k=8 @ n=200 reveal-after early B_k = +3.3pp (full) /
    +5.0pp [+1.7,+8.3] (fresh), excludes parallel/divided/0; contrast +14.8/+11.3 with a sig.+ control →
    "directed ≫ undirected, undirected ≪ divided." Honestly flagged caveats Segment 8 must carry: the
